content.content:
1‚Üí# TJN Home - Customizable Browser Startpage
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíA powerful, CSV-driven browser homepage built on [Tilde](https://github.com/cadejscroggins/tilde) by [Cade Scroggins](https://github.com/cadejscroggins). This enhanced version features dynamic link management via CSV configuration, making it incredibly easy to maintain and customize your personal startpage without touching JavaScript code.
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí## ūüĆü Key Features
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí### CSV-Based Configuration
     8‚Üí- **Effortless Link Management**: Update all your links by editing a simple `data.csv` file
     9‚Üí- **Category Organization**: Group links into logical categories (work, entertainment, shopping, etc.)
    10‚Üí- **Icon Support**: Display custom icons for each link (100+ icons included)
    11‚Üí- **Quick Launch**: Launch multiple sites simultaneously with a single keystroke (`!`)
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí### Smart Search & Navigation
    14‚Üí- **Intelligent Search**: Search with autocomplete powered by DuckDuckGo, search history, and custom suggestions
    15‚Üí- **Site-Specific Search**: Search within specific sites using delimiters (e.g., `g:search term`)
    16‚Üí- **Direct Path Navigation**: Navigate to specific paths on sites (e.g., `r/r/startpages`)
    17‚Üí- **URL Recognition**: Enter any URL or domain for instant redirect
    18‚Üí- **Fallback Search**: Unrecognized queries default to Google or your preferred search engine
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí### Interactive Interface
    21‚Üí- **Clock Display**: Prominent clock showing current time (12 or 24-hour format)
    22‚Üí- **Help Menu**: Press `?` or click the clock to view all available commands
    23‚Üí- **Keyboard-First Design**: Navigate entirely without touching the mouse
    24‚Üí- **Suggestion System**: Real-time suggestions as you type from multiple sources
    25‚Üí- **History Tracking**: Automatically remembers your most frequent searches
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí### Customization
    28‚Üí- **Theme Toggle**: Light/dark mode with reversed color scheme option
    29‚Üí- **Configurable Behavior**: Control new tab behavior, instant redirects, and more
    30‚Üí- **Flexible Search**: Customize search and path delimiters
    31‚Üí- **Responsive Design**: Optimized for desktop and mobile devices
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí## ūüďł Screenshots
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí![Screenshot](assets/screenshot.png)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí## ūüöÄ Quick Start
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí### Option 1: Local File
    40‚Üí1. Download or clone this repository
    41‚Üí2. Open `index.html` in your browser
    42‚Üí3. Set it as your browser's homepage
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí### Option 2: Web Server
    45‚Üí1. Clone the repository to your web server
    46‚Üí2. Access via your domain or IP address
    47‚Üí3. Configure browser homepage to your URL
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí### Option 3: Cloudflare Workers
    50‚Üí```bash
    51‚Üínpm install -g wrangler
    52‚Üíwrangler deploy
    53‚Üí```
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí## ūüďĖ Usage Guide
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí### Basic Commands
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí**Accessing Help**
    60‚Üí- Press `?` to open the help menu
    61‚Üí- Click the clock to open the help menu
    62‚Üí- Press `Escape` to close
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí**Quick Launch**
    65‚Üí- Press `!` to open all sites marked with `quicklaunch: true` in the CSV
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí### Search Syntax
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí**Direct Site Access**
    70‚Üí```
    71‚Üír          ‚Üí Opens reddit.com
    72‚Üíg          ‚Üí Opens github.com
    73‚Üíy          ‚Üí Opens youtube.com
    74‚Üí```
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí**Site-Specific Search** (using `:` delimiter)
    77‚Üí```
    78‚Üíg:tilde-enhanced    ‚Üí Search GitHub for "tilde-enhanced"
    79‚Üíy:cats              ‚Üí Search YouTube for "cats"
    80‚Üír:startpages        ‚Üí Search Reddit for "startpages"
    81‚Üí```
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí**Path Navigation** (using `/` delimiter)
    84‚Üí```
    85‚Üír/r/startpages      ‚Üí Navigate to reddit.com/r/startpages
    86‚Üíh/popular           ‚Üí Navigate to hypem.com/popular
    87‚Üí```
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí**URL Redirect**
    90‚Üí```
    91‚Üíexample.com         ‚Üí Navigate to example.com
    92‚Üíhttps://github.com  ‚Üí Navigate to GitHub
    93‚Üíkeep.google.com     ‚Üí Navigate to Google Keep
    94‚Üí```
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí**Default Search**
    97‚Üí```
    98‚Üícats                ‚Üí Search Google for "cats" (or your configured search engine)
    99‚Üíhow to code         ‚Üí Search Google for "how to code"
   100‚Üí```
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí### Query Parameters
   103‚Üí
   104‚ÜíYou can invoke searches via URL query parameters:
   105‚Üí```
   106‚Üífile:///path/to/index.html?q=cats    ‚Üí Search for "cats"
   107‚Üíhttps://yourdomain.com?q=github      ‚Üí Navigate to GitHub
   108‚Üí```
   109‚Üí
   110‚ÜíThis allows you to use the startpage with your browser's search bar.
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí### Keyboard Shortcuts
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí| Key | Action |
   115‚Üí|-----|--------|
   116‚Üí| `?` | Toggle help menu |
   117‚Üí| `!` | Launch all quick launch sites |
   118‚Üí| `Escape` | Close help menu or clear search |
   119‚Üí| `Enter` | Execute search/command |
   120‚Üí| `Tab` / `Down` / `Ctrl+N` | Next suggestion |
   121‚Üí| `Shift+Tab` / `Up` / `Ctrl+P` | Previous suggestion |
   122‚Üí| Any key | Start typing to search |
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí## ‚öôÔłŹ Configuration
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí### CSV Configuration (`script/data.csv`)
   127‚Üí
   128‚ÜíThe CSV file controls all your links and commands. Each row represents a command with the following columns:
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí| Column | Description | Required | Example |
   131‚Üí|--------|-------------|----------|---------|
   132‚Üí| `category` | Category name (displayed in help menu) | No | `l`, `i`, `m`, `w` |
   133‚Üí| `name` | Display name and command key | Yes | `github`, `reddit`, `youtube` |
   134‚Üí| `url` | Full URL to the site | Yes | `https://github.com` |
   135‚Üí| `color` | Background color for overlay (hex) | No | `#0e0e0e` |
   136‚Üí| `icon` | Icon filename (without .png extension) | No | `github` |
   137‚Üí| `quicklaunch` | Launch with `!` command | No | `true` or empty |
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí**Example CSV Entry:**
   140‚Üí```csv
   141‚Üícategory,name,url,color,icon,quicklaunch
   142‚Üíl,github,https://github.com,,github,
   143‚Üíw,youtube,https://youtube.com,,youtube,true
   144‚Üím,claude,https://claude.ai,,claude,
   145‚Üí```
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí**Category System:**
   148‚ÜíCategories are single characters or short codes that group related links together in the help menu. Common examples:
   149‚Üí- `l` = Links (frequently visited sites)
   150‚Üí- `i` = Information/Learning resources
   151‚Üí- `m` = AI Models/Tools
   152‚Üí- `q` = Shopping (Quick buy)
   153‚Üí- `w` = Watch (Entertainment)
   154‚Üí- `a` = About/Personal links
   155‚Üí- `B` = Backend/Network (internal tools)
   156‚Üí
   157‚ÜíLeave category empty to hide a command from the help menu (still searchable).
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí### JavaScript Configuration (`script/script.js`)
   160‚Üí
   161‚ÜíThe `CONFIG` object at the top of `script.js` controls behavior:
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí```javascript
   164‚Üíconst CONFIG = {
   165‚Üí  commands: [],  // Populated from CSV
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí  // Suggestions
   168‚Üí  suggestions: true,              // Enable autocomplete suggestions
   169‚Üí  suggestionsLimit: 12,          // Maximum suggestions to display
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí  // Influencers control suggestion sources and their priority
   172‚Üí  influencers: [
   173‚Üí    { name: 'Default', limit: 4 },      // Custom suggestions
   174‚Üí    { name: 'History', limit: 1 },       // Search history
   175‚Üí    { name: 'DuckDuckGo', limit: 4 },   // DuckDuckGo API
   176‚Üí  ],
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí  // Default custom suggestions for specific queries
   179‚Üí  defaultSuggestions: {
   180‚Üí    g: ['g/issues', 'g/pulls', 'gist.github.com'],
   181‚Üí    r: ['r/r/unixporn', 'r/r/startpages', 'r/r/webdev'],
   182‚Üí  },
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí  // Behavior
   185‚Üí  instantRedirect: false,   // Redirect immediately when key is matched
   186‚Üí  newTab: true,            // Open results in new tab
   187‚Üí  colors: false,           // Dynamic overlay colors from commands
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí  // Theme
   190‚Üí  reversedColors: false,   // Use light theme instead of dark
   191‚Üí
   192‚Üí  // Delimiters
   193‚Üí  searchDelimiter: ':',    // Character for site search (e.g., g:query)
   194‚Üí  pathDelimiter: '/',      // Character for path navigation (e.g., r/r/sub)
   195‚Üí  clockDelimiter: '',      // Character between clock hours:minutes
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí  // Clock
   198‚Üí  twentyFourHourClock: false,  // Use 24-hour format instead of 12-hour
   199‚Üí}
   200‚Üí```
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí### Icon Management
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí**Adding Custom Icons:**
   205‚Üí1. Add a `.png` icon to `assets/icons/`
   206‚Üí2. Reference it in your CSV (without file extension)
   207‚Üí3. Recommended size: 24x24px or 48x48px (will be scaled)
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí**Icon Naming:**
   210‚Üí- Use lowercase
   211‚Üí- Replace spaces with hyphens or underscores
   212‚Üí- Example: `google-drive.png`, `stack_overflow.png`
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí**100+ Icons Included:**
   215‚ÜíThe project includes icons for popular services like Google, GitHub, YouTube, Reddit, AWS, Azure, and many more. Check `assets/icons/` for the complete list.
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí## ūüŹóÔłŹ Architecture
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí### Project Structure
   220‚Üí
   221‚Üí```
   222‚Üítjn.home/
   223‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ index.html              # Main entry point
   224‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ script/
   225‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ script.js          # Core application logic (~957 lines)
   226‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ parse_csv.js       # CSV parser and data loader
   227‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ data.csv           # Link configuration
   228‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ style/
   229‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ style.css          # Styles and responsive design
   230‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ assets/
   231‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ icons/             # Command icons (100+)
   232‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ fonts/             # Roboto font files
   233‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicons/          # Site favicons and manifest
   234‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ screenshot.png     # Project screenshot
   235‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ wrangler.toml          # Cloudflare Workers config
   236‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ LICENSE                # Unlicense (Public Domain)
   237‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md              # This file
   238‚Üí‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ CLAUDE.md              # Development guidelines
   239‚Üí```
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí### Core Classes
   242‚Üí
   243‚ÜíThe application uses a modular, ES6 class-based architecture:
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí#### **Clock** (`lines 181-226`)
   246‚Üí- Displays current time with AM/PM or 24-hour format
   247‚Üí- Updates every second
   248‚Üí- Toggles help menu on click
   249‚Üí- Configurable delimiter between hours and minutes
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí#### **Help** (`lines 228-354`)
   252‚Üí- Manages help overlay display
   253‚Üí- Builds command categories dynamically from CSV data
   254‚Üí- Handles keyboard events for navigation
   255‚Üí- Supports command updates when CSV is parsed
   256‚Üí- Displays icons for each command
   257‚Üí
   258‚Üí#### **QueryParser** (`lines 638-737`)
   259‚Üí- Parses user input into actionable queries
   260‚Üí- Detects URLs, searches, paths, and direct commands
   261‚Üí- Handles site-specific searches with delimiters
   262‚Üí- Extracts colors from matched commands for overlays
   263‚Üí- Validates and prepares redirect URLs
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí#### **Form** (`lines 739-882`)
   266‚Üí- Manages the search input interface
   267‚Üí- Handles keyboard shortcuts and input events
   268‚Üí- Triggers redirects and searches
   269‚Üí- Integrates with suggester for autocomplete
   270‚Üí- Supports color theme reversal
   271‚Üí- Processes URL query parameters
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí#### **Suggester** (`lines 472-636`)
   274‚Üí- Displays and manages autocomplete suggestions
   275‚Üí- Coordinates multiple influencers for suggestions
   276‚Üí- Handles keyboard navigation through suggestions
   277‚Üí- Tracks user interactions for history
   278‚Üí- Limits suggestion count and highlights matches
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üí#### **Influencers** (`lines 356-470`)
   281‚ÜíSuggestion sources that provide autocomplete data:
   282‚Üí
   283‚Üí- **DefaultInfluencer**: Returns predefined suggestions from CONFIG
   284‚Üí- **DuckDuckGoInfluencer**: Fetches suggestions from DuckDuckGo API via JSONP
   285‚Üí- **HistoryInfluencer**: Tracks and suggests from localStorage-based query history
   286‚Üí
   287‚Üí### Data Flow
   288‚Üí
   289‚Üí1. **Page Load**
   290‚Üí   - `index.html` loads and renders clock
   291‚Üí   - `parse_csv.js` fetches and parses `data.csv`
   292‚Üí   - `csvParsed` event triggers command injection into CONFIG
   293‚Üí   - Help and QueryParser update with new commands
   294‚Üí
   295‚Üí2. **User Input**
   296‚Üí   - User types ‚Üí `Form._handleInput()` triggers
   297‚Üí   - Input sent to `Suggester.suggest()`
   298‚Üí   - Influencers provide suggestions concurrently
   299‚Üí   - Suggestions displayed and highlighted
   300‚Üí
   301‚Üí3. **Command Execution**
   302‚Üí   - User presses Enter ‚Üí `Form._submitForm()` triggers
   303‚Üí   - `QueryParser.parse()` analyzes input
   304‚Üí   - Generates redirect URL based on command type
   305‚Üí   - Opens URL in current or new tab
   306‚Üí   - Adds query to history via influencers
   307‚Üí
   308‚Üí4. **Special Keys**
   309‚Üí   - `?` ‚Üí Toggle help menu
   310‚Üí   - `!` ‚Üí Execute quick launch (opens all flagged sites)
   311‚Üí   - `Escape` ‚Üí Close overlays
   312‚Üí
   313‚Üí### Event System
   314‚Üí
   315‚ÜíThe application uses a custom event-driven architecture:
   316‚Üí
   317‚Üí- **csvParsed**: Fired when CSV data is loaded and parsed
   318‚Üí- **keydown**: Global keyboard event handling for shortcuts
   319‚Üí- **input**: Tracks search input changes for suggestions
   320‚Üí- **submit**: Handles form submission for navigation
   321‚Üí- **click**: Command links and clock interactions
   322‚Üí- **mouseover/mouseout**: Suggestion highlighting
   323‚Üí
   324‚Üí## ūüé® Customization
   325‚Üí
   326‚Üí### Color Theme
   327‚Üí
   328‚Üí**Dark Theme (Default):**
   329‚Üí```css
   330‚Üí--background: #0e0e0e;
   331‚Üí--foreground: #cbcbcb;
   332‚Üí```
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí**Light Theme:**
   335‚ÜíSet `reversedColors: true` in CONFIG or modify CSS variables in `style/style.css`.
   336‚Üí
   337‚Üí**Custom Colors:**
   338‚ÜíUpdate the CSS variables in `style/style.css`:
   339‚Üí```css
   340‚Üí:root {
   341‚Üí  --background: #your-color;
   342‚Üí  --foreground: #your-color;
   343‚Üí  --font-family: 'YourFont', sans-serif;
   344‚Üí  --base-font-size: 12px;
   345‚Üí}
   346‚Üí```
   347‚Üí
   348‚Üí### Search Provider
   349‚Üí
   350‚ÜíChange the default search engine by modifying the `*` command in CONFIG:
   351‚Üí
   352‚Üí```javascript
   353‚Üícommands: [
   354‚Üí  {
   355‚Üí    name: 'Google',
   356‚Üí    key: '*',
   357‚Üí    url: 'https://www.google.com',
   358‚Üí    search: '/search?q={}',
   359‚Üí    color: '#0e0e0e',
   360‚Üí  },
   361‚Üí]
   362‚Üí```
   363‚Üí
   364‚Üí**Alternative Search Engines:**
   365‚Üí- DuckDuckGo: `url: 'https://duckduckgo.com'`, `search: '/?q={}'`
   366‚Üí- Bing: `url: 'https://www.bing.com'`, `search: '/search?q={}'`
   367‚Üí- Brave: `url: 'https://search.brave.com'`, `search: '/search?q={}'`
   368‚Üí- Startpage: `url: 'https://www.startpage.com'`, `search: '/do/search?q={}'`
   369‚Üí
   370‚Üí### Font
   371‚Üí
   372‚ÜíThe project uses Roboto by default (included locally). To change:
   373‚Üí
   374‚Üí1. Add font files to `assets/fonts/`
   375‚Üí2. Update `@font-face` rules in `style/style.css`
   376‚Üí3. Update `--font-family` variable
   377‚Üí
   378‚ÜíOr use a web font:
   379‚Üí```css
   380‚Üí@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;700&display=swap');
   381‚Üí
   382‚Üí:root {
   383‚Üí  --font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
   384‚Üí}
   385‚Üí```
   386‚Üí
   387‚Üí## ūüõ†ÔłŹ Development
   388‚Üí
   389‚Üí### Local Development
   390‚Üí
   391‚Üí1. **Clone the repository:**
   392‚Üí   ```bash
   393‚Üí   git clone https://github.com/travisjneuman/tjn.home.git
   394‚Üí   cd tjn.home
   395‚Üí   ```
   396‚Üí
   397‚Üí2. **Serve locally:**
   398‚Üí   ```bash
   399‚Üí   # Python 3
   400‚Üí   python -m http.server 8000
   401‚Üí
   402‚Üí   # Python 2
   403‚Üí   python -m SimpleHTTPServer 8000
   404‚Üí
   405‚Üí   # Node.js
   406‚Üí   npx serve
   407‚Üí
   408‚Üí   # PHP
   409‚Üí   php -S localhost:8000
   410‚Üí   ```
   411‚Üí
   412‚Üí3. **Open in browser:**
   413‚Üí   ```
   414‚Üí   http://localhost:8000
   415‚Üí   ```
   416‚Üí
   417‚Üí### Adding New Commands
   418‚Üí
   419‚Üí**Method 1: Via CSV (Recommended)**
   420‚Üí1. Open `script/data.csv`
   421‚Üí2. Add a new row with your command details
   422‚Üí3. Refresh the page
   423‚Üí
   424‚Üí**Method 2: Via JavaScript**
   425‚Üí1. Open `script/script.js`
   426‚Üí2. Add to the `commands` array before `// INSERT_PARSING_HERE`
   427‚Üí3. Include: `name`, `key`, `url`, `search` (optional), `color` (optional), `icon` (optional), `category` (optional)
   428‚Üí
   429‚Üí### Modifying Behavior
   430‚Üí
   431‚Üí**Disable Suggestions:**
   432‚Üí```javascript
   433‚Üísuggestions: false,
   434‚Üí```
   435‚Üí
   436‚Üí**Change Suggestion Limit:**
   437‚Üí```javascript
   438‚ÜísuggestionsLimit: 6,
   439‚Üí```
   440‚Üí
   441‚Üí**Instant Redirect:**
   442‚Üí```javascript
   443‚ÜíinstantRedirect: true,  // Navigate immediately when command matches
   444‚Üí```
   445‚Üí
   446‚Üí**Open in Same Tab:**
   447‚Üí```javascript
   448‚ÜínewTab: false,
   449‚Üí```
   450‚Üí
   451‚Üí**Custom Influencer Priority:**
   452‚Üí```javascript
   453‚Üíinfluencers: [
   454‚Üí  { name: 'History', limit: 3 },      // Prioritize history
   455‚Üí  { name: 'Default', limit: 2 },
   456‚Üí  { name: 'DuckDuckGo', limit: 4 },
   457‚Üí],
   458‚Üí```
   459‚Üí
   460‚Üí### Extending Functionality
   461‚Üí
   462‚Üí**Add New Influencer:**
   463‚Üí
   464‚ÜíCreate a class extending `Influencer`:
   465‚Üí```javascript
   466‚Üíclass CustomInfluencer extends Influencer {
   467‚Üí  getSuggestions(query) {
   468‚Üí    return new Promise(resolve => {
   469‚Üí      // Your custom logic
   470‚Üí      const suggestions = ['custom1', 'custom2'];
   471‚Üí      resolve(suggestions.slice(0, this._limit));
   472‚Üí    });
   473‚Üí  }
   474‚Üí}
   475‚Üí```
   476‚Üí
   477‚ÜíRegister in influencers:
   478‚Üí```javascript
   479‚Üíconst influencers = CONFIG.influencers.map(influencerConfig => {
   480‚Üí  return new {
   481‚Üí    Default: DefaultInfluencer,
   482‚Üí    DuckDuckGo: DuckDuckGoInfluencer,
   483‚Üí    History: HistoryInfluencer,
   484‚Üí    Custom: CustomInfluencer,  // Add here
   485‚Üí  }[influencerConfig.name]({...});
   486‚Üí});
   487‚Üí```
   488‚Üí
   489‚Üí**Custom Keyboard Shortcuts:**
   490‚Üí
   491‚ÜíAdd to `Form._handleKeydown()`:
   492‚Üí```javascript
   493‚Üícase 'your-key':
   494‚Üí  // Your custom action
   495‚Üí  return;
   496‚Üí```
   497‚Üí
   498‚Üí## ūüöÄ Deployment
   499‚Üí
   500‚Üí### GitHub Pages
   501‚Üí
   502‚Üí1. Push repository to GitHub
   503‚Üí2. Go to Settings ‚Üí Pages
   504‚Üí3. Select branch (usually `main`) and root directory
   505‚Üí4. Access at `https://yourusername.github.io/tjn.home`
   506‚Üí
   507‚Üí### Cloudflare Pages
   508‚Üí
   509‚Üí1. Connect your GitHub repository
   510‚Üí2. Build settings:
   511‚Üí   - Build command: (none)
   512‚Üí   - Build output directory: `/`
   513‚Üí3. Deploy
   514‚Üí
   515‚Üí### Cloudflare Workers
   516‚Üí
   517‚Üí```bash
   518‚Üínpm install -g wrangler
   519‚Üíwrangler login
   520‚Üíwrangler deploy
   521‚Üí```
   522‚Üí
   523‚Üí### Netlify
   524‚Üí
   525‚Üí1. Connect repository
   526‚Üí2. Build settings:
   527‚Üí   - Build command: (none)
   528‚Üí   - Publish directory: `/`
   529‚Üí3. Deploy
   530‚Üí
   531‚Üí### Self-Hosted
   532‚Üí
   533‚ÜíUpload all files to your web server's document root or subdirectory. No build step or server-side processing required.
   534‚Üí
   535‚Üí**Apache .htaccess (Optional):**
   536‚Üí```apache
   537‚Üí# Force HTTPS
   538‚ÜíRewriteEngine On
   539‚ÜíRewriteCond %{HTTPS} off
   540‚ÜíRewriteRule ^(.*)$ https://%{HTTP_HOST}%{REQUEST_URI} [L,R=301]
   541‚Üí```
   542‚Üí
   543‚Üí**Nginx (Optional):**
   544‚Üí```nginx
   545‚Üíserver {
   546‚Üí    listen 80;
   547‚Üí    server_name yourdomain.com;
   548‚Üí    return 301 https://$server_name$request_uri;
   549‚Üí}
   550‚Üí
   551‚Üíserver {
   552‚Üí    listen 443 ssl http2;
   553‚Üí    server_name yourdomain.com;
   554‚Üí
   555‚Üí    root /var/www/tjn.home;
   556‚Üí    index index.html;
   557‚Üí
   558‚Üí    location / {
   559‚Üí        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
   560‚Üí    }
   561‚Üí}
   562‚Üí```
   563‚Üí
   564‚Üí## ūüĒí Security
   565‚Üí
   566‚ÜíThis project implements multiple security layers to protect against common web vulnerabilities:
   567‚Üí
   568‚Üí### Security Features
   569‚Üí
   570‚Üí**1. HTML Sanitization**
   571‚Üí- All user input and CSV data is sanitized before rendering
   572‚Üí- Prevents Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks
   573‚Üí- Uses `textContent` for safe DOM insertion
   574‚Üí
   575‚Üí**2. Content Security Policy (CSP)**
   576‚Üí- Strict CSP implemented via meta tag
   577‚Üí- Restricts script sources to self and DuckDuckGo only
   578‚Üí- Blocks inline script execution from untrusted sources
   579‚Üí- Prevents data exfiltration
   580‚Üí
   581‚Üí**3. URL Validation**
   582‚Üí- All URLs validated before redirect
   583‚Üí- Only `http:` and `https:` protocols allowed
   584‚Üí- Blocks `javascript:`, `data:`, `file:` protocol attacks
   585‚Üí- Prevents open redirect vulnerabilities
   586‚Üí
   587‚Üí**4. CSV Injection Protection**
   588‚Üí- CSV values sanitized to prevent formula injection
   589‚Üí- Removes dangerous characters (`=`, `+`, `-`, `@`) from cell beginnings
   590‚Üí- Protects against spreadsheet application exploits
   591‚Üí
   592‚Üí**5. JSONP Security**
   593‚Üí- 5-second timeout on external API calls
   594‚Üí- Error handling for failed requests
   595‚Üí- Automatic cleanup of failed script tags
   596‚Üí- Prevents hanging connections
   597‚Üí
   598‚Üí**6. LocalStorage Security**
   599‚Üí- History data validated before storage
   600‚Üí- No sensitive data stored
   601‚Üí- Can be cleared by user at any time
   602‚Üí
   603‚Üí### Security Best Practices
   604‚Üí
   605‚Üí**For CSV Editors**:
   606‚Üí- Never include executable code in CSV fields
   607‚Üí- Avoid starting cells with `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@`
   608‚Üí- Use HTTPS URLs only
   609‚Üí- Validate all URLs before adding to CSV
   610‚Üí
   611‚Üí**For Self-Hosters**:
   612‚Üí- Always use HTTPS in production
   613‚Üí- Keep CSP headers strict
   614‚Üí- Enable HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security)
   615‚Üí- Use secure headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options)
   616‚Üí
   617‚Üí**Example Secure Headers (Nginx)**:
   618‚Üí```nginx
   619‚Üíadd_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
   620‚Üíadd_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
   621‚Üíadd_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
   622‚Üíadd_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
   623‚Üíadd_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
   624‚Üí```
   625‚Üí
   626‚Üí### Vulnerability Disclosure
   627‚Üí
   628‚ÜíIf you discover a security vulnerability, please email: travis@neuman.dev
   629‚Üí
   630‚Üí**Please DO NOT** open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.
   631‚Üí
   632‚Üí## ūüĆź Browser Compatibility
   633‚Üí
   634‚Üí| Browser | Version | Supported |
   635‚Üí|---------|---------|-----------|
   636‚Üí| Chrome | 60+ | ‚úÖ |
   637‚Üí| Firefox | 60+ | ‚úÖ |
   638‚Üí| Safari | 12+ | ‚úÖ |
   639‚Üí| Edge | 79+ | ‚úÖ |
   640‚Üí| Opera | 47+ | ‚úÖ |
   641‚Üí
   642‚Üí**Required Features:**
   643‚Üí- ES6 Classes
   644‚Üí- Fetch API
   645‚Üí- Promises
   646‚Üí- LocalStorage
   647‚Üí- CSS Grid
   648‚Üí- CSS Custom Properties
   649‚Üí- Content Security Policy (CSP) support
   650‚Üí
   651‚Üí## ‚ö° Performance
   652‚Üí
   653‚Üí- **Bundle Size**: ~200KB total (174KB icons + 21KB code, minified)
   654‚Üí- **Icon Optimization**: 131 icons optimized from 19MB ‚Üí 174KB (99.1% reduction)
   655‚Üí- **Code Minification**: JavaScript and CSS minified for production
   656‚Üí- **Load Time**: < 1s on modern connections, < 3s on 3G
   657‚Üí- **No Build Dependencies**: Zero npm packages required to run
   658‚Üí- **Lighthouse Score**: 95+ (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO)
   659‚Üí- **Offline Capable**: Works without internet after initial load (except suggestions)
   660‚Üí
   661‚Üí### Optimization Details
   662‚Üí
   663‚Üí**Icons**:
   664‚Üí- All 131 icons resized to 48x48px maximum
   665‚Üí- Optimized with PNG compression (quality 75)
   666‚Üí- Original 19MB ‚Üí 174KB (99.1% reduction)
   667‚Üí- Largest icons (mgoblog: 6.3MB, reddit: 3.1MB) now < 2KB each
   668‚Üí
   669‚Üí**Code**:
   670‚Üí- JavaScript: 27KB source ‚Üí 16KB minified (40% reduction)
   671‚Üí- CSS: 6.7KB source ‚Üí 5KB minified (25% reduction)
   672‚Üí- All console.log statements removed for production
   673‚Üí- Zero dependencies required at runtime
   674‚Üí
   675‚Üí### Performance Best Practices
   676‚Üí
   677‚Üí**For Self-Hosters**:
   678‚Üí- Enable gzip/brotli compression on your web server
   679‚Üí- Set cache headers for static assets (30 days recommended)
   680‚Üí- Consider CDN for even faster global delivery
   681‚Üí
   682‚Üí**Nginx Example**:
   683‚Üí```nginx
   684‚Üígzip on;
   685‚Üígzip_types text/css application/javascript;
   686‚Üíadd_header Cache-Control "public, max-age=2592000";
   687‚Üí```
   688‚Üí
   689‚Üí**Apache Example**:
   690‚Üí```apache
   691‚Üí<IfModule mod_deflate.c>
   692‚Üí  AddOutputFilterByType DEFLATE text/css application/javascript
   693‚Üí</IfModule>
   694‚Üí<IfModule mod_expires.c>
   695‚Üí  ExpiresActive On
   696‚Üí  ExpiresByType text/css "access plus 30 days"
   697‚Üí  ExpiresByType application/javascript "access plus 30 days"
   698‚Üí</IfModule>
   699‚Üí```
   700‚Üí
   701‚Üí## ūüźõ Troubleshooting
   702‚Üí
   703‚Üí### CSV Not Loading
   704‚Üí
   705‚Üí**Issue**: Commands from CSV don't appear
   706‚Üí
   707‚Üí**Solutions**:
   708‚Üí1. Check browser console for fetch errors
   709‚Üí2. Ensure CSV path is correct: `/script/data.csv`
   710‚Üí3. Verify CSV format (proper commas, no BOM)
   711‚Üí4. Clear browser cache
   712‚Üí5. Check file permissions if self-hosting
   713‚Üí
   714‚Üí### Icons Not Displaying
   715‚Üí
   716‚Üí**Issue**: Command icons don't show
   717‚Üí
   718‚Üí**Solutions**:
   719‚Üí1. Verify icon exists in `assets/icons/`
   720‚Üí2. Check icon filename matches CSV (case-sensitive, without .png)
   721‚Üí3. Ensure icon format is PNG
   722‚Üí4. Check browser console for 404 errors
   723‚Üí
   724‚Üí### Search Not Working
   725‚Üí
   726‚Üí**Issue**: Searches don't redirect properly
   727‚Üí
   728‚Üí**Solutions**:
   729‚Üí1. Check search delimiter in CONFIG (default `:`)
   730‚Üí2. Verify command has a `search` property
   731‚Üí3. Ensure search URL contains `{}` placeholder
   732‚Üí4. Test with default search by typing random text
   733‚Üí
   734‚Üí### Quick Launch Doesn't Work
   735‚Üí
   736‚Üí**Issue**: `!` key doesn't launch sites
   737‚Üí
   738‚Üí**Solutions**:
   739‚Üí1. Enable pop-ups in browser settings
   740‚Üí2. Verify CSV has `quicklaunch` column set to `true` (lowercase)
   741‚Üí3. Check browser console for errors
   742‚Üí4. Test with a single quick launch site first
   743‚Üí
   744‚Üí### Suggestions Not Appearing
   745‚Üí
   746‚Üí**Issue**: Autocomplete doesn't show
   747‚Üí
   748‚Üí**Solutions**:
   749‚Üí1. Verify `suggestions: true` in CONFIG
   750‚Üí2. Check internet connection (DuckDuckGo requires network)
   751‚Üí3. Look for JSONP/CORS errors in console
   752‚Üí4. Try disabling ad blockers (may block DuckDuckGo API)
   753‚Üí
   754‚Üí### History Not Saving
   755‚Üí
   756‚Üí**Issue**: Search history doesn't persist
   757‚Üí
   758‚Üí**Solutions**:
   759‚Üí1. Ensure localStorage is enabled in browser
   760‚Üí2. Check if in private/incognito mode
   761‚Üí3. Verify localStorage isn't full (5-10MB limit)
   762‚Üí4. Test in different browser
   763‚Üí
   764‚Üí## ūüďĚ License
   765‚Üí

   777‚Üí## ūüôŹ Credits

   780‚Üí- **[Tilde](https://github.com/cadejscroggins/tilde)** by [Cade Scroggins](https://github.com/cadejscroggins)

   796‚Üí## ūü§Ě Contributing
   797‚Üí
   798‚ÜíContributions are welcome! This is a personal project, but feel free to:
   799‚Üí

file.content:
# TJN Home - Customizable Browser Startpage

A powerful, CSV-driven browser homepage built on [Tilde](https://github.com/cadejscroggins/tilde) by [Cade Scroggins](https://github.com/cadejscroggins). This enhanced version features dynamic link management via CSV configuration, making it incredibly easy to maintain and customize your personal startpage without touching JavaScript code.

## ūüĆü Key Features

### CSV-Based Configuration
- **Effortless Link Management**: Update all your links by editing a simple `data.csv` file
- **Category Organization**: Group links into logical categories (work, entertainment, shopping, etc.)
- **Icon Support**: Display custom icons for each link (100+ icons included)
- **Quick Launch**: Launch multiple sites simultaneously with a single keystroke (`!`)

### Smart Search & Navigation
- **Intelligent Search**: Search with autocomplete powered by DuckDuckGo, search history, and custom suggestions
- **Site-Specific Search**: Search within specific sites using delimiters (e.g., `g:search term`)
- **Direct Path Navigation**: Navigate to specific paths on sites (e.g., `r/r/startpages`)
- **URL Recognition**: Enter any URL or domain for instant redirect
- **Fallback Search**: Unrecognized queries default to Google or your preferred search engine

### Interactive Interface
- **Clock Display**: Prominent clock showing current time (12 or 24-hour format)
- **Help Menu**: Press `?` or click the clock to view all available commands
- **Keyboard-First Design**: Navigate entirely without touching the mouse
- **Suggestion System**: Real-time suggestions as you type from multiple sources
- **History Tracking**: Automatically remembers your most frequent searches

### Customization
- **Theme Toggle**: Light/dark mode with reversed color scheme option
- **Configurable Behavior**: Control new tab behavior, instant redirects, and more
- **Flexible Search**: Customize search and path delimiters
- **Responsive Design**: Optimized for desktop and mobile devices

## ūüďĖ Usage Guide

### Basic Commands

**Accessing Help**
- Press `?` to open the help menu
- Click the clock to open the help menu
- Press `Escape` to close

**Quick Launch**
- Press `!` to open all sites marked with `quicklaunch: true` in the CSV

### Search Syntax

**Direct Site Access**
```
r          ‚Üí Opens reddit.com
g          ‚Üí Opens github.com
y          ‚Üí Opens youtube.com
```

**Site-Specific Search** (using `:` delimiter)
```
g:tilde-enhanced    ‚Üí Search GitHub for "tilde-enhanced"
y:cats              ‚Üí Search YouTube for "cats"
r:startpages        ‚Üí Search Reddit for "startpages"
```

**Path Navigation** (using `/` delimiter)
```
r/r/startpages      ‚Üí Navigate to reddit.com/r/startpages
h/popular           ‚Üí Navigate to hypem.com/popular
```

**URL Redirect**
```
example.com         ‚Üí Navigate to example.com
https://github.com  ‚Üí Navigate to GitHub
keep.google.com     ‚Üí Navigate to Google Keep
```

**Default Search**
```
cats                ‚Üí Search Google for "cats" (or your configured search engine)
how to code         ‚Üí Search Google for "how to code"
```

### Query Parameters

You can invoke searches via URL query parameters:
```
file:///path/to/index.html?q=cats    ‚Üí Search for "cats"
https://yourdomain.com?q=github      ‚Üí Navigate to GitHub
```

This allows you to use the startpage with your browser's search bar.

### Keyboard Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `?` | Toggle help menu |
| `!` | Launch all quick launch sites |
| `Escape` | Close help menu or clear search |
| `Enter` | Execute search/command |
| `Tab` / `Down` / `Ctrl+N` | Next suggestion |
| `Shift+Tab` / `Up` / `Ctrl+P` | Previous suggestion |
| Any key | Start typing to search |

## ‚öôÔłŹ Configuration

### CSV Configuration (`script/data.csv`)

The CSV file controls all your links and commands. Each row represents a command with the following columns:

| Column | Description | Required | Example |
|--------|-------------|----------|---------|
| `category` | Category name (displayed in help menu) | No | `l`, `i`, `m`, `w` |
| `name` | Display name and command key | Yes | `github`, `reddit`, `youtube` |
| `url` | Full URL to the site | Yes | `https://github.com` |
| `color` | Background color for overlay (hex) | No | `#0e0e0e` |
| `icon` | Icon filename (without .png extension) | No | `github` |
| `quicklaunch` | Launch with `!` command | No | `true` or empty |

**Example CSV Entry:**
```csv
category,name,url,color,icon,quicklaunch
l,github,https://github.com,,github,
w,youtube,https://youtube.com,,youtube,true
m,claude,https://claude.ai,,claude,
```

**Category System:**
Categories are single characters or short codes that group related links together in the help menu. Common examples:
- `l` = Links (frequently visited sites)
- `i` = Information/Learning resources
- `m` = AI Models/Tools
- `q` = Shopping (Quick buy)
- `w` = Watch (Entertainment)
- `a` = About/Personal links
- `B` = Backend/Network (internal tools)

Leave category empty to hide a command from the help menu (still searchable).

### JavaScript Configuration (`script/script.js`)

The `CONFIG` object at the top of `script.js` controls behavior:

```javascript
const CONFIG = {
  commands: [],  // Populated from CSV

  // Suggestions
  suggestions: true,              // Enable autocomplete suggestions
  suggestionsLimit: 12,          // Maximum suggestions to display

  // Influencers control suggestion sources and their priority
  influencers: [
    { name: 'Default', limit: 4 },      // Custom suggestions
    { name: 'History', limit: 1 },       // Search history
    { name: 'DuckDuckGo', limit: 4 },   // DuckDuckGo API
  ],

  // Default custom suggestions for specific queries
  defaultSuggestions: {
    g: ['g/issues', 'g/pulls', 'gist.github.com'],
    r: ['r/r/unixporn', 'r/r/startpages', 'r/r/webdev'],
  },

  // Behavior
  instantRedirect: false,   // Redirect immediately when key is matched
  newTab: true,            // Open results in new tab
  colors: false,           // Dynamic overlay colors from commands

  // Theme
  reversedColors: false,   // Use light theme instead of dark

  // Delimiters
  searchDelimiter: ':',    // Character for site search (e.g., g:query)
  pathDelimiter: '/',      // Character for path navigation (e.g., r/r/sub)
  clockDelimiter: '',      // Character between clock hours:minutes

  // Clock
  twentyFourHourClock: false,  // Use 24-hour format instead of 12-hour
}
```

### Icon Management

**Adding Custom Icons:**
1. Add a `.png` icon to `assets/icons/`
2. Reference it in your CSV (without file extension)
3. Recommended size: 24x24px or 48x48px (will be scaled)

**Icon Naming:**
- Use lowercase
- Replace spaces with hyphens or underscores
- Example: `google-drive.png`, `stack_overflow.png`

**100+ Icons Included:**
The project includes icons for popular services like Google, GitHub, YouTube, Reddit, AWS, Azure, and many more. Check `assets/icons/` for the complete list.

## ūüŹóÔłŹ Architecture

### Project Structure

```
tjn.home/
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ index.html              # Main entry point
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ script/
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ script.js          # Core application logic (~957 lines)
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ parse_csv.js       # CSV parser and data loader
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ data.csv           # Link configuration
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ style/
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ style.css          # Styles and responsive design
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ assets/
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ icons/             # Command icons (100+)
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ fonts/             # Roboto font files
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicons/          # Site favicons and manifest
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ screenshot.png     # Project screenshot
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ wrangler.toml          # Cloudflare Workers config
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ LICENSE                # Unlicense (Public Domain)
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md              # This file
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ CLAUDE.md              # Development guidelines
```

### Core Classes

The application uses a modular, ES6 class-based architecture:

#### **Clock** (`lines 181-226`)
- Displays current time with AM/PM or 24-hour format
- Updates every second
- Toggles help menu on click
- Configurable delimiter between hours and minutes

#### **Help** (`lines 228-354`)
- Manages help overlay display
- Builds command categories dynamically from CSV data
- Handles keyboard events for navigation
- Supports command updates when CSV is parsed
- Displays icons for each command

#### **QueryParser** (`lines 638-737`)
- Parses user input into actionable queries
- Detects URLs, searches, paths, and direct commands
- Handles site-specific searches with delimiters
- Extracts colors from matched commands for overlays
- Validates and prepares redirect URLs

#### **Form** (`lines 739-882`)
- Manages the search input interface
- Handles keyboard shortcuts and input events
- Triggers redirects and searches
- Integrates with suggester for autocomplete
- Supports color theme reversal
- Processes URL query parameters

#### **Suggester** (`lines 472-636`)
- Displays and manages autocomplete suggestions
- Coordinates multiple influencers for suggestions
- Handles keyboard navigation through suggestions
- Tracks user interactions for history
- Limits suggestion count and highlights matches

#### **Influencers** (`lines 356-470`)
Suggestion sources that provide autocomplete data:

- **DefaultInfluencer**: Returns predefined suggestions from CONFIG
- **DuckDuckGoInfluencer**: Fetches suggestions from DuckDuckGo API via JSONP
- **HistoryInfluencer**: Tracks and suggests from localStorage-based query history

### Data Flow

1. **Page Load**
   - `index.html` loads and renders clock
   - `parse_csv.js` fetches and parses `data.csv`
   - `csvParsed` event triggers command injection into CONFIG
   - Help and QueryParser update with new commands

2. **User Input**
   - User types ‚Üí `Form._handleInput()` triggers
   - Input sent to `Suggester.suggest()`
   - Influencers provide suggestions concurrently
   - Suggestions displayed and highlighted

3. **Command Execution**
   - User presses Enter ‚Üí `Form._submitForm()` triggers
   - `QueryParser.parse()` analyzes input
   - Generates redirect URL based on command type
   - Opens URL in current or new tab
   - Adds query to history via influencers

4. **Special Keys**
   - `?` ‚Üí Toggle help menu
   - `!` ‚Üí Execute quick launch (opens all flagged sites)
   - `Escape` ‚Üí Close overlays

### Event System

The application uses a custom event-driven architecture:

- **csvParsed**: Fired when CSV data is loaded and parsed
- **keydown**: Global keyboard event handling for shortcuts
- **input**: Tracks search input changes for suggestions
- **submit**: Handles form submission for navigation
- **click**: Command links and clock interactions
- **mouseover/mouseout**: Suggestion highlighting

commands: [

### Extending Functionality

**Add New Influencer:**

Create a class extending `Influencer`:
```javascript
class CustomInfluencer extends Influencer {
  getSuggestions(query) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      // Your custom logic
      const suggestions = ['custom1', 'custom2'];
      resolve(suggestions.slice(0, this._limit));
    });
  }
}
```

Register in influencers:
```javascript
const influencers = CONFIG.influencers.map(influencerConfig => {
  return new {
    Default: DefaultInfluencer,
    DuckDuckGo: DuckDuckGoInfluencer,
    History: HistoryInfluencer,
    Custom: CustomInfluencer,  // Add here
  }[influencerConfig.name]({...});
});
```

**Custom Keyboard Shortcuts:**

Add to `Form._handleKeydown()`:
```javascript
case 'your-key':
  // Your custom action
  return;
```

## ūüĒí Security

This project implements multiple security layers to protect against common web vulnerabilities:

### Security Features

**1. HTML Sanitization**
- All user input and CSV data is sanitized before rendering
- Prevents Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks
- Uses `textContent` for safe DOM insertion

**2. Content Security Policy (CSP)**
- Strict CSP implemented via meta tag
- Restricts script sources to self and DuckDuckGo only
- Blocks inline script execution from untrusted sources
- Prevents data exfiltration

**3. URL Validation**
- All URLs validated before redirect
- Only `http:` and `https:` protocols allowed
- Blocks `javascript:`, `data:`, `file:` protocol attacks
- Prevents open redirect vulnerabilities

**4. CSV Injection Protection**
- CSV values sanitized to prevent formula injection
- Removes dangerous characters (`=`, `+`, `-`, `@`) from cell beginnings
- Protects against spreadsheet application exploits

**5. JSONP Security**
- 5-second timeout on external API calls
- Error handling for failed requests
- Automatic cleanup of failed script tags
- Prevents hanging connections

**6. LocalStorage Security**
- History data validated before storage
- No sensitive data stored
- Can be cleared by user at any time

### Security Best Practices

**For CSV Editors**:
- Never include executable code in CSV fields
- Avoid starting cells with `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@`
- Use HTTPS URLs only
- Validate all URLs before adding to CSV

**For Self-Hosters**:
- Always use HTTPS in production
- Keep CSP headers strict
- Enable HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security)
- Use secure headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options)

**Example Secure Headers (Nginx)**:
```nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
```

### Vulnerability Disclosure

If you discover a security vulnerability, please email: travis@neuman.dev

**Please DO NOT** open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.

- **Icon Optimization**: 131 icons optimized from 19MB ‚Üí 174KB (99.1% reduction)
- **Code Minification**: JavaScript and CSS minified for production
- **Load Time**: < 1s on modern connections, < 3s on 3G
- **No Build Dependencies**: Zero npm packages required to run
- **Lighthouse Score**: 95+ (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO)

### Optimization Details

**Icons**:
- All 131 icons resized to 48x48px maximum
- Optimized with PNG compression (quality 75)
- Original 19MB ‚Üí 174KB (99.1% reduction)
- Largest icons (mgoblog: 6.3MB, reddit: 3.1MB) now < 2KB each

### Performance Best Practices

**For Self-Hosters**:
- Enable gzip/brotli compression on your web server
- Set cache headers for static assets (30 days recommended)

**Nginx Example**:
```nginx

```

## ūüźõ Troubleshooting

### CSV Not Loading

**Issue**: Commands from CSV don't appear

**Solutions**:
1. Check browser console for fetch errors
2. Ensure CSV path is correct: `/script/data.csv`
3. Verify CSV format (proper commas, no BOM)
4. Clear browser cache
5. Check file permissions if self-hosting

### Icons Not Displaying

**Issue**: Command icons don't show

**Solutions**:
1. Verify icon exists in `assets/icons/`
2. Check icon filename matches CSV (case-sensitive, without .png)
3. Ensure icon format is PNG
4. Check browser console for 404 errors

### Search Not Working

**Issue**: Searches don't redirect properly

**Solutions**:
1. Check search delimiter in CONFIG (default `:`)
2. Verify command has a `search` property
3. Ensure search URL contains `{}` placeholder
4. Test with default search by typing random text

### Quick Launch Doesn't Work

**Issue**: `!` key doesn't launch sites

**Solutions**:
1. Enable pop-ups in browser settings
2. Verify CSV has `quicklaunch` column set to `true` (lowercase)
3. Check browser console for errors
4. Test with a single quick launch site first

### Suggestions Not Appearing

**Issue**: Autocomplete doesn't show

**Solutions**:
1. Verify `suggestions: true` in CONFIG
2. Check internet connection (DuckDuckGo requires network)
3. Look for JSONP/CORS errors in console
4. Try disabling ad blockers (may block DuckDuckGo API)

### History Not Saving

**Issue**: Search history doesn't persist

**Solutions**:
1. Ensure localStorage is enabled in browser
2. Check if in private/incognito mode
3. Verify localStorage isn't full (5-10MB limit)
4. Test in different browser
