    16‚Üí- **Privacy:** local‚ÄĎfirst; no PII collection; no server data exfil; **E2EE** backups/exports. Anonymous analytics allowed (Cloudflare Web Analytics - no cookies, no fingerprinting, GDPR-compliant). Geolocation prompts user (not blocked).
    17‚Üí- **Security:** sanitize inputs; never render user content via `innerHTML`; prefer **WebCrypto**; no secrets committed.
    18‚Üí- **Coherence:** follow existing patterns (Zustand stores, Widget system, Tailwind semantic tokens). No competing paradigms.
    19‚Üí- **Atomic delivery:** small diffs; explain *why*; avoid big‚ÄĎbang refactors.
    20‚Üí- **Maintainability:** explicit > clever; minimal "magic".
    21‚Üí- **Ownership:** build features custom, use utility libraries only (see "Build vs Buy" below). Must be commercially viable (MIT/Apache/BSD licensed).
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí---
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí## Context hygiene (token/quality control)
    26‚Üí- Prefer short, high‚ÄĎsignal summaries over long logs. If output is huge, summarize + attach only the relevant excerpt.
    27‚Üí- Don't `@`-embed large docs by default. Instead: reference paths + *when* to read them.
    28‚Üí- Use `/clear` after finishing a small unit of work (or when the thread gets noisy) to keep context clean; rely on this file + task files for continuity.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí### Smart Delegation (tache features)
    31‚ÜíWhen context gets heavy (>100k tokens) or tasks are moderate complexity (30 min - 2 hours):
    32‚Üí- Use **`/create-prompt`** to create delegatable prompt ‚Üí `/run-prompt` to execute in fresh context
    33‚Üí- Main context stays clean, avoids token bloat
    34‚Üí- Results integrated back after completion
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíFor multi-stage features requiring research ‚Üí plan ‚Üí implement:
    37‚Üí- Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** to create pipeline ‚Üí `/run-prompt` delegates entire workflow
    38‚Üí- Each stage produces output for next stage; context remains pristine
    39‚Üí
    40‚ÜíWhen approaching session boundaries:
    41‚Üí- Use **`/whats-next`** to create handoff document ‚Üí `/clear` to start fresh
    42‚Üí- Next session resumes with handoff context; no information loss
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíSee [Tache Integration Guide](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md) for complete workflow patterns.
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí---
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí## Session protocol (strict)
    49‚Üí### Start (in order)
    50‚Üí1. `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`
    51‚Üí2. Read `<env>` for **today's date** (never guess dates).
    52‚Üí3. `git log -3`
    53‚Üí4. **`/check-todos`** to list outstanding work (or `ls tasks/*.md` for explicit task files)
    54‚Üí5. If resuming: read task file and active todos; if new work: describe naturally, let routing determine workflow
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí### End (in order)
    57‚Üí1. `npm run test && npm run type-check`
    58‚Üí2. If feature behavior changed/added: update `docs/product/feature_map.md`
    59‚Üí3. Archive completed work:
    60‚Üí   - Tasks: `mv tasks/<task>.md .archive/completed-tasks/`
    61‚Üí   - Prompts: `mv prompts/<prompt>.md .archive/completed-prompts/`
    62‚Üí4. If created/modified custom skills/commands/agents: use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, or **`/audit-subagent`** before committing
    63‚Üí5. Use **`/add-to-todos`** to capture any follow-up items discovered during work
    64‚Üí6. Commit with a comprehensive message.
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíDev server rule: start `npm run dev` **only** to test UI changes; stop immediately after.
    67‚Üí- Verify stopped: `lsof -ti:5173` (macOS/Linux) OR `Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173` / `netstat -ano | findstr :5173` (Windows).
    68‚Üí
    69‚ÜíBackground process rule: kill all background shells (Playwright tests, long-running scripts, etc.) when done.
    70‚Üí- Check running shells: Use `/tasks` command or monitor system reminders
    71‚Üí- Kill shells: Use `KillShell` tool with shell_id
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí---
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí## Workflow (default)
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí### Intelligent Prompt Routing (AUTOMATIC)
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí**ALL prompts are automatically analyzed and routed to optimal tache features based on:**
    80‚Üí- **Keywords**: "plan", "debug", "should we", "create", "audit" ‚Üí specific features
    81‚Üí- **Complexity**: Simple (1-2 files) ‚Üí direct; Moderate (3-10 files) ‚Üí delegate; Complex (multi-stage) ‚Üí pipeline
    82‚Üí- **Type**: Planning ‚Üí `/create-plan`; Debugging ‚Üí `/debug` or `debug-like-expert`; Decisions ‚Üí `/consider:*` frameworks
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí**Routing is transparent‚ÄĒjust describe what you need naturally.**
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**Common routing patterns:**
    87‚Üí| Prompt Type | Auto-Routes To | Example |
    88‚Üí|-------------|----------------|---------|
    89‚Üí| "Plan the X feature" | `/create-plan` | Creates hierarchical plan with phases |
    90‚Üí| "Debug intermittent Y" | `debug-like-expert` skill | Systematic root cause analysis |
    91‚Üí| "Should we use A or B?" | Appropriate `/consider:*` | Structured decision framework |
    92‚Üí| "Refactor across N files" | `/create-prompt` + `/run-prompt` | Delegates to fresh context |
    93‚Üí| "Research then implement X" | `/create-meta-prompt` | Multi-stage pipeline |
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíFull routing specification: [Tache Integration Guide ¬ß Intelligent Routing](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#part-2-intelligent-routing-system)
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí---
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí### Standard Workflow (for direct execution or after routing)
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí1. **Plan:** For complex work, routing auto-suggests `/create-plan`. Otherwise create `tasks/<descriptive-name>.md` (stage early). Include:
   102‚Üí   - Overview + requirements
   103‚Üí   - Files to read/modify/create
   104‚Üí   - Ordered atomic checklist
   105‚Üí   - Key decisions + rationale
   106‚Üí   - (Optional) time estimate
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí2. **Approval gate:** **must use `ExitPlanMode` before any code edits/writes**.
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí3. **Execute:** use `TodoWrite`; mark items as you finish them; keep commits atomic.
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí4. **Verify:** automated + manual checks for impacted UX flows.
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí5. **Close out:** add task "Review" section (what changed, verification, issues).
   115‚Üí
   116‚ÜíTask template (if present): `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   117‚Üí
   118‚ÜíPre‚ÄĎcommit: commits are blocked when code changes without a staged task file (see `.husky/pre-commit`).
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí---
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üí## Stack (constraints)
   123‚Üí- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7
   124‚Üí- Zustand 5 (persist middleware)
   125‚Üí- Tailwind 3 **semantic tokens** (no hardcoded colors)
   126‚Üí- IndexedDB via Dexie (local persistence)
   127‚Üí- React Router 7
   128‚Üí- Editors/embeds: Lexical (notes), XTerm (terminal)
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí---
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí## Build vs Buy (dependency philosophy)
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí**Core Principle:** We build features, we use utilities. Never copy-paste complete feature implementations.
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí**What We Build (90%+ ownership):**
   137‚Üí- All feature logic, business rules, UI/UX
   138‚Üí- All data models, persistence, exports
   139‚Üí- All integrations with our stores/services
   140‚Üí- All user-facing functionality
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí**What We Use (Utilities):**
   143‚Üí- **Allowed:** Libraries that provide low-level abstractions/algorithms
   144‚Üí  - Examples: D3 (graph layout), Recharts (chart primitives), Lexical (rich text editing), Konva (canvas rendering), XTerm (terminal emulation)
   145‚Üí  - Criterion: We own the feature, library handles complexity we shouldn't reimplement
   146‚Üí- **NOT Allowed:** Complete feature implementations
   147‚Üí  - Examples: Full Excalidraw (diagram editor), Trello clone (task board), complete calendar apps
   148‚Üí  - Criterion: If we're wrapping/extending their feature instead of building our own, it's wrong
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí**License Requirements:**
   151‚Üí- **Must use:** MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD licenses (commercial use allowed)
   152‚Üí- **Never use:** GPL, AGPL (copyleft - blocks future commercialization)
   153‚Üí- **Verify:** All dependencies must allow selling the platform for profit
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí**Decision Framework:**
   156‚ÜíWhen considering a new dependency, ask:
   157‚Üí1. **Am I building or wrapping?** If wrapping ‚Üí reject, build custom
   158‚Üí2. **What % is my code?** If <80% ‚Üí reject, build custom or use smaller utility
   159‚Üí3. **Can I sell this?** If license blocks commercial use ‚Üí reject
   160‚Üí4. **Can I fork if needed?** If closed-source or unmaintained ‚Üí reject
   161‚Üí5. **Bundle size?** If >100KB for single feature ‚Üí reject or find smaller alternative
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí**Examples:**
   164‚Üí- ‚úÖ **D3 for Graph View:** We use D3's force simulation (utility), we built the entire Graph feature
   165‚Üí- ‚úÖ **Konva for Diagrams:** We use Konva for canvas (utility), we built the entire Diagram editor
   166‚Üí- ‚úÖ **Recharts for Analytics:** We use Recharts primitives (utility), we built the Analytics feature
   167‚Üí- ‚ĚĆ **Full Excalidraw:** Would be 90% their code, 10% ours - this is wrong
   168‚Üí- ‚ĚĆ **Trello library:** Would be wrapping their task board, not building ours
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí**Pride & Control Test:**
   171‚ÜíCan we truthfully say "We built this feature" and have full control to add any capability we want? If no, wrong approach.
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üí---
   174‚Üí
   175‚Üí## Commands (known scripts)
   176‚Üí- Dev: `npm run dev` (port 5173)
   177‚Üí- Build: `npm run build`, `npm run build:production` (if present)
   178‚Üí- Test: `npm run test`, `npm run test:ui` (if present), `npm run test:coverage` (if present)
   179‚Üí- Types: `npm run type-check`
   180‚Üí- Quality: `npm run lint`, `npm run format`, `npm run preview` (if present)
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí### Testing Strategy
   183‚Üí**Unit Tests (Vitest):** Run automatically in CI on every push. Fast, reliable, comprehensive coverage.
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí**E2E Tests (Playwright):** Run **locally only**, not in CI. Rationale:
   186‚Üí- Local-first app architecture = testing where app runs (locally) makes most sense
   187‚Üí- No server/deployment to verify in CI
   188‚Üí- E2E tests more valuable during local development
   189‚Üí- Avoids CI flakiness and overhead
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí**Before committing:** Run `npm run test && npm run type-check` locally. Optionally run `npx playwright test` for E2E verification of critical flows.
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí---
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí## Performance budgets (targets; justify exceptions)
   196‚Üí- **Entry/initial route chunk:** < **100KB gz** (stretch goal).
   197‚Üí- **Total initial load:** keep as low as possible via lazy routes/widgets; explain tradeoffs when exceeded.
   198‚Üí- Must use lazy-loading for heavy pages/widgets and prefer named imports (tree‚ÄĎshaking).
   199‚Üí
   200‚ÜíUX targets: <1s load where feasible; <100ms interaction latency where feasible; WCAG AA minimum.
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí---
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí## Folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (read when working in that area)
   205‚Üí| Folder | CLAUDE.md | Focus |
   206‚Üí|--------|-----------|-------|
   207‚Üí| `src/hooks/` | `CLAUDE.md` | React hooks patterns + inventory |
   208‚Üí| `src/pages/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Route pages + lazy loading |
   209‚Üí| `src/services/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Business logic + service patterns |
   210‚Üí| `src/stores/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Zustand stores + persistence |
   211‚Üí| `src/widgets/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Widget system + registry |
   212‚Üí| `src/components/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Shared UI components |
   213‚Üí| `tests/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Testing patterns (Vitest/Playwright) |
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí**IMPORTANT:** Always read the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when working in that area. These contain domain-specific patterns, inventories, and constraints.
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí### When to update folder CLAUDE.md files
   218‚ÜíUpdate the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when:
   219‚Üí- **Adding a new file:** Add it to the inventory table
   220‚Üí- **Removing a file:** Remove from inventory table
   221‚Üí- **Changing patterns:** Update the pattern section if conventions change
   222‚Üí- **New anti-patterns discovered:** Add to anti-patterns list
   223‚Üí- **Breaking changes:** Document in non-negotiables if it affects the folder
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí**Maintenance triggers:**
   226‚Üí- Created new hook ‚Üí update `src/hooks/CLAUDE.md` inventory
   227‚Üí- Created new store ‚Üí update `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` inventory
   228‚Üí- Created new widget ‚Üí update `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` categories
   229‚Üí- Created new service ‚Üí update `src/services/CLAUDE.md` inventory
   230‚Üí- Created new page ‚Üí update `src/pages/CLAUDE.md` inventory
   231‚Üí- Created new component ‚Üí update `src/components/CLAUDE.md` categories
   232‚Üí- Added E2E test ‚Üí update `tests/CLAUDE.md` if new pattern established
   233‚Üí
   234‚Üí---
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí## Repo map (high‚ÄĎsignal orientation)
   237‚Üí- **Pages:** `src/pages/*` ‚ÄĒ lazy routes (Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, LinkLibrary, Automations, Settings)
   238‚Üí- **State:** `src/stores/*` ‚ÄĒ Zustand + persist (no Redux)
   239‚Üí- **Widgets:** `src/widgets/Dashboard/*` ‚ÄĒ `BaseWidget.tsx` wrapper, `WidgetRegistry.ts` discovery
   240‚Üí- **Services:** `src/services/*` ‚ÄĒ business logic (`indexedDB.ts`, `automationEngine.ts`, `brainBackup.ts`, `validation.ts`, `ai/*`)
   241‚Üí- **Components:** `src/components/*` ‚ÄĒ shared UI (modals, layout, calendar views, etc.)
   242‚Üí- **Hooks:** `src/hooks/*` ‚ÄĒ custom React hooks
   243‚Üí- **Tests:** `src/**/__tests__/*` (Vitest), `tests/e2e/*` (Playwright)
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí---
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí## Critical invariants (do not break)
   248‚Üí- **Date keys:** `YYYY-M-D` (NOT zero‚ÄĎpadded) used across Calendar/TimeTracking/Events.
   249‚Üí- Automation:
   250‚Üí  - Event triggers include `task.created`, `task.moved`, `task.completed`, `task.updated`
   251‚Üí  - Recursion/loop protection: max depth 10
   252‚Üí- Schema changes: migrations must be reversible; backup/restore must round-trip without loss.
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí---
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí## Thinking Frameworks (structured decision-making)
   257‚Üí
   258‚ÜíWhen making architectural or strategic decisions, use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for systematic analysis:
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí| Decision Type | Framework | When to Use |
   261‚Üí|---------------|-----------|-------------|
   262‚Üí| Long-term implications | `/consider:10-10-10` | Tech choices, migrations, architecture |
   263‚Üí| Root cause analysis | `/consider:5-whys` | Recurring problems, understanding patterns |
   264‚Üí| Prioritization | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` | Backlog triage, sprint planning |
   265‚Üí| Innovation | `/consider:first-principles` | Rethinking approaches, ground-up design |
   266‚Üí| Risk identification | `/consider:inversion` | Security review, failure mode analysis |
   267‚Üí| Simplification | `/consider:occams-razor` | Choosing between approaches |
   268‚Üí| Focus | `/consider:one-thing` | Identifying critical path |
   269‚Üí| Tradeoffs | `/consider:opportunity-cost` | Resource allocation, build vs. buy |
   270‚Üí| Optimization | `/consider:pareto` | 80/20 analysis, high-value focus |
   271‚Üí| Consequences | `/consider:second-order` | API changes, breaking changes |
   272‚Üí| Evaluation | `/consider:swot` | Technology assessment, strategic planning |
   273‚Üí| Reduction | `/consider:via-negativa` | Refactoring, complexity removal |
   274‚Üí
   275‚ÜíFull reference: [Tache Integration Guide ¬ß Thinking Frameworks](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#33-thinking-frameworks-consider-commands)
   276‚Üí
   277‚Üí---
   278‚Üí
   279‚Üí## Tooling policy (Claude Code)
   280‚Üí
   281‚Üí### File/Code Operations
   282‚Üí- Use **Read** for known paths; **Grep/Glob** for symbol search.
   283‚Üí- Use **Task/subagents** only for multi-step exploration/research or cross-cutting reviews.
   284‚Üí
   285‚Üí### Development Workflows
   286‚Üí- Use **`/create-plan`** for planning complex features (>1 phase)
   287‚Üí- Use **`/debug`** for clear bugs; **`debug-like-expert` skill** for intermittent/mysterious issues
   288‚Üí- Use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for architectural decisions (see Thinking Frameworks above)
   289‚Üí- Use **`/create-prompt`** for delegating moderate tasks (30 min - 2 hours) to fresh context
   290‚Üí- Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** for multi-stage features (research ‚Üí plan ‚Üí implement)
   291‚Üí
   292‚Üí### Decision-Making
   293‚Üí- Use **AskUserQuestion** when requirements are ambiguous or multiple architectures are valid
   294‚Üí- Use appropriate **`/consider:*` framework** for structured decisions (not ad-hoc analysis)
   295‚Üí
   296‚Üí### Code Creation & Modification
   297‚Üí- Use **ExitPlanMode** before edits/writes
   298‚Üí- Use **`/create-agent-skill`** for custom skills (project-specific capabilities)
   299‚Üí- Use **`/create-slash-command`** for project commands (repetitive workflows)
   300‚Üí- Use **`/create-subagent`** for specialized agents (domain experts)
   301‚Üí
   302‚Üí### Quality Assurance
   303‚Üí- Use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, **`/audit-subagent`** before committing
   304‚Üí- Use **`/heal-skill`** to fix issues discovered during skill execution
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí### Verification
   307‚Üí- Browser/E2E: Playwright (and Playwright MCP when available) for Chrome verification of critical flows
   308‚Üí- Run: `npm run test && npm run type-check` before committing
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üí---
   311‚Üí
   312‚Üí## Docs: authority + drift control
   313‚ÜíAuthority order (highest ‚Üí lowest):
   314‚Üí1. `docs/technical/development_principles.md`
   315‚Üí2. `docs/technical/widget_development_guide.md`
   316‚Üí3. `docs/technical/api_integration_guide.md`
   317‚Üí4. `docs/product/feature_map.md`
   318‚Üí5. `tasks/master-implementation-status.md` (roadmap/status)
   319‚Üí6. `.claude/skills/README.md`
   320‚Üí7. `README.md`
   321‚Üí
   322‚ÜíIf docs conflict: higher authority wins; update stale docs.
   323‚Üí
   324‚Üí---
   325‚Üí
   326‚Üí## Git conventions
   327‚Üí- Prefer conventional commit types: `feat|fix|refactor|perf|test|docs|chore`.
   328‚Üí- Do not push to `main` without review unless explicitly instructed.
   329‚Üí- Pre-commit bypass (`git commit --no-verify`) is emergency-only; document why in the task file.
   330‚Üí
   331‚ÜíRepo issues: `https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues`
   332‚Üí
   333‚Üí---
   334‚Üí
   335‚Üí## Anti‚ÄĎpatterns (do not do)
   336‚Üí- Missing/unstaged task file when code changes.
   337‚Üí- Generic task names (`tasks/todo.md`, `tasks/task.md`, etc.).
   338‚Üí- Batch completing todos; update as you go.
   339‚Üí- Scope creep without asking.
   340‚Üí- Guessing dates; always read `<env>`.
   341‚Üí- Leaving dev server running.
   342‚Üí- Leaving background shells running (Playwright tests, etc.).
   343‚Üí- Using `any` (use explicit types or `unknown`).
   344‚Üí- Hardcoded Tailwind colors (use semantic tokens).
   345‚Üí- Ignoring folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (always read when working in that folder).
   346‚Üí- Forcing direct execution for complex features (use `/create-plan` or `/create-meta-prompt`).
   347‚Üí- Letting context bloat (use `/create-prompt` to delegate moderate tasks to fresh context).
   348‚Üí- Manual decision-making when `/consider:*` frameworks exist (use frameworks for structure).
   349‚Üí- Debugging intermittent issues manually (use `debug-like-expert` for systematic approach).
   350‚Üí- Creating custom skills/commands/agents without audit (use `/audit-*` before committing).

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md ‚ÄĒ Neuman Brain (constitution, token‚ÄĎlean)

Purpose: persistent repo‚ÄĎspecific rules for Claude Code. Keep this file lean; put deep/volatile details in folder‚ÄĎlevel `CLAUDE.md` files.

## Identity
Role: lead autonomous engineer/architect for **Neuman Brain** (privacy‚ÄĎfirst, local‚ÄĎonly productivity dashboard).

Core mindset: project is ~1% complete ‚Üí build primitives that scale, fix root causes, repair ‚Äúbroken windows‚ÄĚ (stale docs/code) when encountered.

Communication: direct, technical. Prefer correctness over speed.

---

## P0 (non‚ÄĎnegotiable)
- **Data integrity:** never lose user data; handle failures; migrations reversible.
- **Privacy:** local‚ÄĎfirst; no PII collection; no server data exfil; **E2EE** backups/exports. Anonymous analytics allowed (Cloudflare Web Analytics - no cookies, no fingerprinting, GDPR-compliant). Geolocation prompts user (not blocked).
- **Security:** sanitize inputs; never render user content via `innerHTML`; prefer **WebCrypto**; no secrets committed.
- **Coherence:** follow existing patterns (Zustand stores, Widget system, Tailwind semantic tokens). No competing paradigms.
- **Atomic delivery:** small diffs; explain *why*; avoid big‚ÄĎbang refactors.
- **Maintainability:** explicit > clever; minimal "magic".
- **Ownership:** build features custom, use utility libraries only (see "Build vs Buy" below). Must be commercially viable (MIT/Apache/BSD licensed).

---

## Context hygiene (token/quality control)
- Prefer short, high‚ÄĎsignal summaries over long logs. If output is huge, summarize + attach only the relevant excerpt.
- Don't `@`-embed large docs by default. Instead: reference paths + *when* to read them.
- Use `/clear` after finishing a small unit of work (or when the thread gets noisy) to keep context clean; rely on this file + task files for continuity.

### Smart Delegation (tache features)
When context gets heavy (>100k tokens) or tasks are moderate complexity (30 min - 2 hours):
- Use **`/create-prompt`** to create delegatable prompt ‚Üí `/run-prompt` to execute in fresh context
- Main context stays clean, avoids token bloat
- Results integrated back after completion

For multi-stage features requiring research ‚Üí plan ‚Üí implement:
- Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** to create pipeline ‚Üí `/run-prompt` delegates entire workflow
- Each stage produces output for next stage; context remains pristine

When approaching session boundaries:
- Use **`/whats-next`** to create handoff document ‚Üí `/clear` to start fresh
- Next session resumes with handoff context; no information loss

See [Tache Integration Guide](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md) for complete workflow patterns.

---

## Session protocol (strict)
### Start (in order)
1. `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`
2. Read `<env>` for **today's date** (never guess dates).
3. `git log -3`
4. **`/check-todos`** to list outstanding work (or `ls tasks/*.md` for explicit task files)
5. If resuming: read task file and active todos; if new work: describe naturally, let routing determine workflow

### End (in order)
1. `npm run test && npm run type-check`
2. If feature behavior changed/added: update `docs/product/feature_map.md`
3. Archive completed work:
   - Tasks: `mv tasks/<task>.md .archive/completed-tasks/`
   - Prompts: `mv prompts/<prompt>.md .archive/completed-prompts/`
4. If created/modified custom skills/commands/agents: use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, or **`/audit-subagent`** before committing
5. Use **`/add-to-todos`** to capture any follow-up items discovered during work
6. Commit with a comprehensive message.

Dev server rule: start `npm run dev` **only** to test UI changes; stop immediately after.
- Verify stopped: `lsof -ti:5173` (macOS/Linux) OR `Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173` / `netstat -ano | findstr :5173` (Windows).

Background process rule: kill all background shells (Playwright tests, long-running scripts, etc.) when done.
- Check running shells: Use `/tasks` command or monitor system reminders
- Kill shells: Use `KillShell` tool with shell_id

---

## Workflow (default)

### Intelligent Prompt Routing (AUTOMATIC)

**ALL prompts are automatically analyzed and routed to optimal tache features based on:**
- **Keywords**: "plan", "debug", "should we", "create", "audit" ‚Üí specific features
- **Complexity**: Simple (1-2 files) ‚Üí direct; Moderate (3-10 files) ‚Üí delegate; Complex (multi-stage) ‚Üí pipeline
- **Type**: Planning ‚Üí `/create-plan`; Debugging ‚Üí `/debug` or `debug-like-expert`; Decisions ‚Üí `/consider:*` frameworks

**Routing is transparent‚ÄĒjust describe what you need naturally.**

**Common routing patterns:**
| Prompt Type | Auto-Routes To | Example |
|-------------|----------------|---------|
| "Plan the X feature" | `/create-plan` | Creates hierarchical plan with phases |
| "Debug intermittent Y" | `debug-like-expert` skill | Systematic root cause analysis |
| "Should we use A or B?" | Appropriate `/consider:*` | Structured decision framework |
| "Refactor across N files" | `/create-prompt` + `/run-prompt` | Delegates to fresh context |
| "Research then implement X" | `/create-meta-prompt` | Multi-stage pipeline |

Full routing specification: [Tache Integration Guide ¬ß Intelligent Routing](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#part-2-intelligent-routing-system)

---

### Standard Workflow (for direct execution or after routing)

1. **Plan:** For complex work, routing auto-suggests `/create-plan`. Otherwise create `tasks/<descriptive-name>.md` (stage early). Include:
   - Overview + requirements
   - Files to read/modify/create
   - Ordered atomic checklist
   - Key decisions + rationale
   - (Optional) time estimate

2. **Approval gate:** **must use `ExitPlanMode` before any code edits/writes**.

3. **Execute:** use `TodoWrite`; mark items as you finish them; keep commits atomic.

4. **Verify:** automated + manual checks for impacted UX flows.

5. **Close out:** add task "Review" section (what changed, verification, issues).

Task template (if present): `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Pre‚ÄĎcommit: commits are blocked when code changes without a staged task file (see `.husky/pre-commit`).

---

## Stack (constraints)
- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7
- Zustand 5 (persist middleware)
- Tailwind 3 **semantic tokens** (no hardcoded colors)
- IndexedDB via Dexie (local persistence)
- React Router 7
- Editors/embeds: Lexical (notes), XTerm (terminal)

---

## Build vs Buy (dependency philosophy)

**Core Principle:** We build features, we use utilities. Never copy-paste complete feature implementations.

**What We Build (90%+ ownership):**
- All feature logic, business rules, UI/UX
- All data models, persistence, exports
- All integrations with our stores/services
- All user-facing functionality

**What We Use (Utilities):**
- **Allowed:** Libraries that provide low-level abstractions/algorithms
  - Examples: D3 (graph layout), Recharts (chart primitives), Lexical (rich text editing), Konva (canvas rendering), XTerm (terminal emulation)
  - Criterion: We own the feature, library handles complexity we shouldn't reimplement
- **NOT Allowed:** Complete feature implementations
  - Examples: Full Excalidraw (diagram editor), Trello clone (task board), complete calendar apps
  - Criterion: If we're wrapping/extending their feature instead of building our own, it's wrong

**License Requirements:**
- **Must use:** MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD licenses (commercial use allowed)
- **Never use:** GPL, AGPL (copyleft - blocks future commercialization)
- **Verify:** All dependencies must allow selling the platform for profit

**Decision Framework:**
When considering a new dependency, ask:
1. **Am I building or wrapping?** If wrapping ‚Üí reject, build custom
2. **What % is my code?** If <80% ‚Üí reject, build custom or use smaller utility
3. **Can I sell this?** If license blocks commercial use ‚Üí reject
4. **Can I fork if needed?** If closed-source or unmaintained ‚Üí reject
5. **Bundle size?** If >100KB for single feature ‚Üí reject or find smaller alternative

**Examples:**
- ‚úÖ **D3 for Graph View:** We use D3's force simulation (utility), we built the entire Graph feature
- ‚úÖ **Konva for Diagrams:** We use Konva for canvas (utility), we built the entire Diagram editor
- ‚úÖ **Recharts for Analytics:** We use Recharts primitives (utility), we built the Analytics feature
- ‚ĚĆ **Full Excalidraw:** Would be 90% their code, 10% ours - this is wrong
- ‚ĚĆ **Trello library:** Would be wrapping their task board, not building ours

**Pride & Control Test:**
Can we truthfully say "We built this feature" and have full control to add any capability we want? If no, wrong approach.

---

## Commands (known scripts)
- Dev: `npm run dev` (port 5173)
- Build: `npm run build`, `npm run build:production` (if present)
- Test: `npm run test`, `npm run test:ui` (if present), `npm run test:coverage` (if present)
- Types: `npm run type-check`
- Quality: `npm run lint`, `npm run format`, `npm run preview` (if present)

### Testing Strategy
**Unit Tests (Vitest):** Run automatically in CI on every push. Fast, reliable, comprehensive coverage.

**E2E Tests (Playwright):** Run **locally only**, not in CI. Rationale:
- Local-first app architecture = testing where app runs (locally) makes most sense
- No server/deployment to verify in CI
- E2E tests more valuable during local development
- Avoids CI flakiness and overhead

**Before committing:** Run `npm run test && npm run type-check` locally. Optionally run `npx playwright test` for E2E verification of critical flows.

---

## Performance budgets (targets; justify exceptions)
- **Entry/initial route chunk:** < **100KB gz** (stretch goal).
- **Total initial load:** keep as low as possible via lazy routes/widgets; explain tradeoffs when exceeded.
- Must use lazy-loading for heavy pages/widgets and prefer named imports (tree‚ÄĎshaking).

UX targets: <1s load where feasible; <100ms interaction latency where feasible; WCAG AA minimum.

---

## Folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (read when working in that area)
| Folder | CLAUDE.md | Focus |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| `src/hooks/` | `CLAUDE.md` | React hooks patterns + inventory |
| `src/pages/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Route pages + lazy loading |
| `src/services/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Business logic + service patterns |
| `src/stores/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Zustand stores + persistence |
| `src/widgets/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Widget system + registry |
| `src/components/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Shared UI components |
| `tests/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Testing patterns (Vitest/Playwright) |

**IMPORTANT:** Always read the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when working in that area. These contain domain-specific patterns, inventories, and constraints.

### When to update folder CLAUDE.md files
Update the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when:
- **Adding a new file:** Add it to the inventory table
- **Removing a file:** Remove from inventory table
- **Changing patterns:** Update the pattern section if conventions change
- **New anti-patterns discovered:** Add to anti-patterns list
- **Breaking changes:** Document in non-negotiables if it affects the folder

**Maintenance triggers:**
- Created new hook ‚Üí update `src/hooks/CLAUDE.md` inventory
- Created new store ‚Üí update `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` inventory
- Created new widget ‚Üí update `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` categories
- Created new service ‚Üí update `src/services/CLAUDE.md` inventory
- Created new page ‚Üí update `src/pages/CLAUDE.md` inventory
- Created new component ‚Üí update `src/components/CLAUDE.md` categories
- Added E2E test ‚Üí update `tests/CLAUDE.md` if new pattern established

---

## Repo map (high‚ÄĎsignal orientation)
- **Pages:** `src/pages/*` ‚ÄĒ lazy routes (Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, LinkLibrary, Automations, Settings)
- **State:** `src/stores/*` ‚ÄĒ Zustand + persist (no Redux)
- **Widgets:** `src/widgets/Dashboard/*` ‚ÄĒ `BaseWidget.tsx` wrapper, `WidgetRegistry.ts` discovery
- **Services:** `src/services/*` ‚ÄĒ business logic (`indexedDB.ts`, `automationEngine.ts`, `brainBackup.ts`, `validation.ts`, `ai/*`)
- **Components:** `src/components/*` ‚ÄĒ shared UI (modals, layout, calendar views, etc.)
- **Hooks:** `src/hooks/*` ‚ÄĒ custom React hooks
- **Tests:** `src/**/__tests__/*` (Vitest), `tests/e2e/*` (Playwright)

---

## Critical invariants (do not break)
- **Date keys:** `YYYY-M-D` (NOT zero‚ÄĎpadded) used across Calendar/TimeTracking/Events.
- Automation:
  - Event triggers include `task.created`, `task.moved`, `task.completed`, `task.updated`
  - Recursion/loop protection: max depth 10
- Schema changes: migrations must be reversible; backup/restore must round-trip without loss.

---

## Thinking Frameworks (structured decision-making)

When making architectural or strategic decisions, use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for systematic analysis:

| Decision Type | Framework | When to Use |
|---------------|-----------|-------------|
| Long-term implications | `/consider:10-10-10` | Tech choices, migrations, architecture |
| Root cause analysis | `/consider:5-whys` | Recurring problems, understanding patterns |
| Prioritization | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` | Backlog triage, sprint planning |
| Innovation | `/consider:first-principles` | Rethinking approaches, ground-up design |
| Risk identification | `/consider:inversion` | Security review, failure mode analysis |
| Simplification | `/consider:occams-razor` | Choosing between approaches |
| Focus | `/consider:one-thing` | Identifying critical path |
| Tradeoffs | `/consider:opportunity-cost` | Resource allocation, build vs. buy |
| Optimization | `/consider:pareto` | 80/20 analysis, high-value focus |
| Consequences | `/consider:second-order` | API changes, breaking changes |
| Evaluation | `/consider:swot` | Technology assessment, strategic planning |
| Reduction | `/consider:via-negativa` | Refactoring, complexity removal |

Full reference: [Tache Integration Guide ¬ß Thinking Frameworks](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#33-thinking-frameworks-consider-commands)

---

## Tooling policy (Claude Code)

### File/Code Operations
- Use **Read** for known paths; **Grep/Glob** for symbol search.
- Use **Task/subagents** only for multi-step exploration/research or cross-cutting reviews.

### Development Workflows
- Use **`/create-plan`** for planning complex features (>1 phase)
- Use **`/debug`** for clear bugs; **`debug-like-expert` skill** for intermittent/mysterious issues
- Use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for architectural decisions (see Thinking Frameworks above)
- Use **`/create-prompt`** for delegating moderate tasks (30 min - 2 hours) to fresh context
- Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** for multi-stage features (research ‚Üí plan ‚Üí implement)

### Decision-Making
- Use **AskUserQuestion** when requirements are ambiguous or multiple architectures are valid
- Use appropriate **`/consider:*` framework** for structured decisions (not ad-hoc analysis)

### Code Creation & Modification
- Use **ExitPlanMode** before edits/writes
- Use **`/create-agent-skill`** for custom skills (project-specific capabilities)
- Use **`/create-slash-command`** for project commands (repetitive workflows)
- Use **`/create-subagent`** for specialized agents (domain experts)

### Quality Assurance
- Use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, **`/audit-subagent`** before committing
- Use **`/heal-skill`** to fix issues discovered during skill execution

### Verification
- Browser/E2E: Playwright (and Playwright MCP when available) for Chrome verification of critical flows
- Run: `npm run test && npm run type-check` before committing

---

## Docs: authority + drift control
Authority order (highest ‚Üí lowest):
1. `docs/technical/development_principles.md`
2. `docs/technical/widget_development_guide.md`
3. `docs/technical/api_integration_guide.md`
4. `docs/product/feature_map.md`
5. `tasks/master-implementation-status.md` (roadmap/status)
6. `.claude/skills/README.md`
7. `README.md`

If docs conflict: higher authority wins; update stale docs.

---

## Git conventions
- Prefer conventional commit types: `feat|fix|refactor|perf|test|docs|chore`.
- Do not push to `main` without review unless explicitly instructed.
- Pre-commit bypass (`git commit --no-verify`) is emergency-only; document why in the task file.

Repo issues: `https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues`

---

## Anti‚ÄĎpatterns (do not do)
- Missing/unstaged task file when code changes.
- Generic task names (`tasks/todo.md`, `tasks/task.md`, etc.).
- Batch completing todos; update as you go.
- Scope creep without asking.
- Guessing dates; always read `<env>`.
- Leaving dev server running.
- Leaving background shells running (Playwright tests, etc.).
- Using `any` (use explicit types or `unknown`).
- Hardcoded Tailwind colors (use semantic tokens).
- Ignoring folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (always read when working in that folder).
- Forcing direct execution for complex features (use `/create-plan` or `/create-meta-prompt`).
- Letting context bloat (use `/create-prompt` to delegate moderate tasks to fresh context).
- Manual decision-making when `/consider:*` frameworks exist (use frameworks for structure).
- Debugging intermittent issues manually (use `debug-like-expert` for systematic approach).
- Creating custom skills/commands/agents without audit (use `/audit-*` before committing).

   155‚Üí## ‚ö†ÔłŹ Critical Development Priorities
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí> **These principles are NON-NEGOTIABLE.** Every decision, feature, and line of code must align with these priorities. When in conflict, priorities higher in the list take precedence.
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí### Priority Hierarchy (In Order)
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí#### 1. ūüĒí SECURITY (Non-Negotiable)
   162‚Üí- **Defense in depth** - Multiple layers of protection, never rely on single control
   163‚Üí- **Never trust user input** - Validate, sanitize, escape at every boundary
   164‚Üí- **Secure by default** - New features must be secure out of the box
   165‚Üí- **Zero tolerance for secrets in code** - No API keys, tokens, passwords in source
   166‚Üí- **OWASP Top 10 awareness** - Actively prevent XSS, injection, CSRF, auth issues
   167‚Üí- **Data minimization** - Only collect/store what's absolutely necessary
   168‚Üí- **Audit logging** - Track security-relevant actions for forensics
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí#### 2. ūüŹóÔłŹ FOUNDATIONAL (Build Right)
   171‚Üí- **Solid architecture first** - No shortcuts that create tech debt
   172‚Üí- **Single source of truth** - One authoritative location for every piece of data
   173‚Üí- **Clean integration** - New features must integrate with existing systems cleanly
   174‚Üí- **Backward compatibility** - Don't break existing functionality without migration path
   175‚Üí- **Database integrity** - Proper constraints, indexes, foreign keys, cascades
   176‚Üí
   177‚Üí#### 3. ūüõ°ÔłŹ STABILITY (Never Crash)
   178‚Üí- **Zero tolerance for crashes** - Comprehensive error handling everywhere
   179‚Üí- **No data loss** - Every user action either succeeds completely or fails safely
   180‚Üí- **Graceful degradation** - Features fail gracefully when dependencies unavailable
   181‚Üí- **Test critical paths** - Auth, payments, data mutations must have test coverage
   182‚Üí- **Defensive programming** - Assume inputs are malformed, APIs are down, state is corrupted
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí#### 4. ‚ú® UX/UI (User Delight)
   185‚Üí- **Speed is a feature** - Target <100ms perceived interaction latency
   186‚Üí- **Mobile-first responsive** - Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
   187‚Üí- **Intuitive flows** - Users should never wonder "what do I do next?"
   188‚Üí- **Accessibility required** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard nav, screen readers
   189‚Üí- **Consistent design language** - Miami Vice theme applied uniformly
   190‚Üí- **Feedback on every action** - Loading states, success messages, error explanations
   191‚Üí- **Forgiveness** - Easy undo, confirmation for destructive actions
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí#### 5. ūüéĮ LEAN (Ship Minimal)
   194‚Üí- **MVP first** - Ship the minimum viable solution, iterate based on feedback
   195‚Üí- **No feature creep** - If it's not solving a real user problem, don't build it
   196‚Üí- **Question every dependency** - Each package adds risk, size, and maintenance burden
   197‚Üí- **Simpler is better** - Fewer abstractions, less code, easier maintenance
   198‚Üí- **Delete before adding** - Remove unused code before adding new features
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí#### 6. ūüßĻ CLEAN (Readable Code)
   201‚Üí- **Readable > clever** - Anyone should understand code without deep context
   202‚Üí- **Consistent naming** - Follow established conventions throughout
   203‚Üí- **Single responsibility** - Each function/component does one thing well
   204‚Üí- **No dead code** - Remove unused imports, functions, files immediately
   205‚Üí- **Self-documenting** - Code explains "what", comments explain "why"
   206‚Üí
   207‚Üí#### 7. ūüďą RETENTION (Bring Users Back)
   208‚Üí- **Valuable data** - Help users track stats they can't get elsewhere
   209‚Üí- **Progress visualization** - Show improvement over time
   210‚Üí- **Social features** - Playing partners, comparisons, friendly competition
   211‚Üí- **Low friction** - One-tap round logging, smart defaults, remember preferences
   212‚Üí- **Notifications done right** - Valuable reminders, never spam
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí#### 8. ‚ö° EFFICIENT + OPTIMIZED (Performance)
   215‚Üí- **Measure first** - Profile before optimizing, no premature optimization
   216‚Üí- **Performance budgets** - Set and enforce bundle size limits
   217‚Üí- **Core Web Vitals** - LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
   218‚Üí- **Database optimization** - Proper indexes, query analysis, N+1 prevention
   219‚Üí- **Caching strategy** - Cache at every layer (CDN, API, database, client)
   220‚Üí- **Lazy loading** - Load code and data only when needed
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí#### 9. ūüĒĄ UPDATED + KNOWLEDGEABLE (Stay Current)
   223‚Üí- **Current dependencies** - Regular updates, security patches applied promptly
   224‚Üí- **Modern patterns** - Use current best practices, avoid deprecated approaches
   225‚Üí- **Understand before changing** - Read existing code thoroughly before modifying
   226‚Üí- **Know the "why"** - Understand reasoning behind architectural decisions
   227‚Üí- **Documentation current** - CLAUDE.md and docs updated with every change
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí#### 10. ūüŹĆÔłŹ INDUSTRY-FAMILIAR (Golf Domain Expertise)
   230‚Üí- **Handicap systems** - GHIN, WHS, course/slope ratings, handicap index calculation
   231‚Üí- **Golf terminology** - Correct use of GIR, FIR, putts per round, scoring terms
   232‚Üí- **Course data** - Par, rating, slope, tee boxes, layouts
   233‚Üí- **Tournament formats** - Stroke play, match play, Stableford, scramble
   234‚Üí- **Competitor awareness** - Know what GHIN, Golf Pad, 18Birdies offer
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí---
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üí### Golden Rules (Never Break)
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
   241‚Üí2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
   242‚Üí3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`**
   243‚Üí4. **Admin routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`**
   244‚Üí5. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
   245‚Üí6. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens
   246‚Üí7. **Test before deploy** - No untested code reaches production
   247‚Üí8. **Mobile-first** - Every feature must work on mobile devices
   248‚Üí
   249‚Üí### Before Completing ANY Task
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí**Quick checklist** (see `@docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí- [ ] **Security:** No vulnerabilities (injection, XSS, auth bypass, secrets)
   254‚Üí- [ ] **Stability:** Error handling for all failure modes
   255‚Üí- [ ] **UX:** Works on mobile, accessible, has loading/error states
   256‚Üí- [ ] **Lean:** No unnecessary code, dependencies, or features
   257‚Üí- [ ] **Clean:** Follows existing patterns, readable, no dead code
   258‚Üí- [ ] **Tests:** `npm run test` (backend) / `npm run build` (frontend) pass
   259‚Üí- [ ] **Docs:** CLAUDE.md updated if patterns changed
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí### CSV Data Workflow
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí```bash
   264‚Üí# 1. Edit CSV file
   265‚Üívim golfers/golfers.csv
   266‚Üí
   267‚Üí# 2. Regenerate JSON
   268‚Üínpm run build:data
   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí# 3. Verify
   271‚Üínpm run check:data
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí# 4. Commit BOTH files
   274‚Üígit add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
   275‚Üígit commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
   276‚Üí```
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí---
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üí## Architecture Overview
   281‚Üí
   282‚Üí### Frontend Data Flow
   283‚Üí
   284‚Üí```
   285‚ÜíCSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
   286‚Üígolfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
   287‚Üíleagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
   288‚Üítours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
   289‚Üí```
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí### Backend Module Structure
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üí```
   294‚Üísrc/
   295‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
   296‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
   297‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
   298‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ courses/        # Course management
   299‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ prisma/         # Database service (global module)
   300‚Üí‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ audit-log/      # Action logging
   301‚Üí```
   302‚Üí
   303‚Üí### Database Models
   304‚Üí
   305‚Üí| Model | Description |
   306‚Üí|-------|-------------|
   307‚Üí| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
   308‚Üí| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
   309‚Üí| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
   310‚Üí| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
   311‚Üí| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
   312‚Üí| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
   313‚Üí| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |
   314‚Üí
   315‚Üí### Authentication Flow
   316‚Üí
   317‚Üí1. Frontend ‚Üí `GET /auth/google` ‚Üí Google consent
   318‚Üí2. Google ‚Üí Backend callback with code
   319‚Üí3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
   320‚Üí4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
   321‚Üí5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
   322‚Üí6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm
   323‚Üí
   324‚Üí---
   325‚Üí
   326‚Üí## Environment Variables
   327‚Üí
   328‚Üí### Backend (.env)
   329‚Üí
   330‚Üí```ini
   331‚ÜíPORT=3000
   332‚ÜíNODE_ENV=development|production
   333‚ÜíDATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
   334‚ÜíJWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
   335‚ÜíGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
   336‚ÜíGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
   337‚ÜíGOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
   338‚Üí```
   339‚Üí
   340‚Üí### CORS Origins (src/main.ts)
   341‚Üí
   342‚Üí```
   343‚Üíhttps://lazygolfing.com
   344‚Üíhttps://www.lazygolfing.com
   345‚Üíhttps://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev
   346‚Üí```
   347‚Üí
   348‚ÜíFor local development, add your origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).
   349‚Üí
   350‚Üí---
   351‚Üí
   352‚Üí## Troubleshooting Quick Reference
   353‚Üí
   354‚Üí| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
   355‚Üí|---------|-------|----------|
   356‚Üí| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
   357‚Üí| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
   358‚Üí| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
   359‚Üí| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
   360‚Üí| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
   361‚Üí| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |
   362‚Üí
   363‚Üí---
   364‚Üí
   365‚Üí## Documentation Links
   366‚Üí
   367‚Üí### Development Resources
   368‚Üí
   369‚Üí| Document | Description |
   370‚Üí|----------|-------------|
   371‚Üí| [Master Todo List](./docs/todo/MASTER_TODO.md) | Task tracking with priorities |
   372‚Üí| [Technical Debt Register](./docs/technical-debt/DEBT_REGISTER.md) | Code quality issues |

   374‚Üí| [Database Schema](./docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md) | Prisma models & ER diagram |
   375‚Üí
   376‚Üí### Architecture Resources
   377‚Üí
   378‚Üí| Document | Description |
   379‚Üí|----------|-------------|
   380‚Üí| [System Overview](./docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) | High-level architecture |

   382‚Üí| [Competitive Landscape](./docs/research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md) | 11-category market analysis |
   383‚Üí
   384‚Üí### Planning Resources
   385‚Üí
   386‚Üí| Document | Description |
   387‚Üí|----------|-------------|
   388‚Üí| [Product Roadmap](./docs/plans/PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md) | Vision, timeline, success metrics |
   389‚Üí| [Feature Roadmap](./docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) | Comprehensive feature list |
   390‚Üí
   391‚Üí### Project-Specific Guides
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí| Document | Description |
   394‚Üí|----------|-------------|
   395‚Üí| [Frontend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.home/CLAUDE.md) | Next.js 15, React 19, components |
   396‚Üí| [Backend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) | NestJS 11, Prisma, authentication |
   397‚Üí
   398‚Üí---
   399‚Üí
   400‚Üí## AI Working Principles
   401‚Üí
   402‚Üí### Approach
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
   405‚Üí2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
   406‚Üí3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
   407‚Üí4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs
   408‚Üí
   409‚Üí### When to Ask
   410‚Üí
   411‚Üí- Requirements are ambiguous
   412‚Üí- Security implications unclear
   413‚Üí- Breaking changes needed
   414‚Üí- Performance trade-offs exist
   415‚Üí
   416‚Üí### When to Proceed
   417‚Üí
   418‚Üí- Implementation matches existing patterns
   419‚Üí- Non-breaking additions
   420‚Üí- Bug fixes with clear root cause
   421‚Üí- Refactoring that preserves behavior
   422‚Üí
   423‚Üí---
   424‚Üí
   425‚Üí## Brand Guidelines
   426‚Üí
   427‚Üí### Voice
   428‚Üí- Tagline: "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"
   429‚Üí- Friendly, inclusive, self-aware
   430‚Üí- Celebrate small wins and funny losses
   431‚Üí
   432‚Üí### Miami Vice Color Palette
   433‚Üí
   434‚Üí| Color | Hex | Theme Usage |
   435‚Üí|-------|-----|-------------|
   436‚Üí| Hot Pink | #ff0080 | Dark mode primary |
   437‚Üí| Cyan | #00f0ff | Dark mode secondary |
   438‚Üí| Purple | #9b59ff | Dark mode accent |
   439‚Üí| Coral | #ff6b6b | Light mode primary |
   440‚Üí| Orange | #ff7033 | Light mode secondary |
   441‚Üí| Yellow | #ffbf00 | Light mode accent |
   442‚Üí
   443‚Üí**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`
   444‚Üí
   445‚ÜíSee [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) for full documentation.
   446‚Üí
   447‚Üí---
   448‚Üí
   449‚Üí## Workspace Organization Rules
   450‚Üí
   451‚ÜíThese rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.
   452‚Üí
   453‚Üí### Single Source of Truth Principle
   454‚Üí
   455‚ÜíEvery piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:
   456‚Üí
   457‚Üí| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
   458‚Üí|------|----------------|-------------------|
   459‚Üí| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
   460‚Üí| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
   461‚Üí| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
   462‚Üí| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |
   463‚Üí
   464‚Üí### Static Asset Organization
   465‚Üí
   466‚ÜíAll static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:
   467‚Üí
   468‚Üí```
   469‚Üípublic/
   470‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicons/     # All favicon files
   471‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
   472‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
   473‚Üí‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
   474‚Üí```
   475‚Üí
   476‚Üí**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).
   477‚Üí
   478‚Üí### Social Sharing Standards
   479‚Üí
   480‚Üí| Standard | Specification |
   481‚Üí|----------|--------------|
   482‚Üí| OG Image Size | 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio) |
   483‚Üí| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
   484‚Üí| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
   485‚Üí| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |
   486‚Üí
   487‚Üí### Documentation Hierarchy
   488‚Üí
   489‚Üí```
   490‚Üí/CLAUDE.md                    # Quick reference, critical rules
   491‚Üí/docs/                        # Detailed documentation
   492‚Üí  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ plans/                  # Roadmaps, feature lists
   493‚Üí  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ research/               # Market research, competitors
   494‚Üí  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ technical-debt/         # Code quality tracking
   495‚Üí  ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ architecture/           # System design
   496‚Üí/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md          # Frontend-specific rules
   497‚Üí/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md       # Backend-specific rules
   498‚Üí```
   499‚Üí
   500‚Üí### Naming Conventions
   501‚Üí
   502‚Üí**Quick reference** (see `@docs/guidelines/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md` for complete guide):
   503‚Üí
   504‚Üí| Type | Convention | Example |
   505‚Üí|------|------------|---------|
   506‚Üí| React Components | PascalCase | `Header.tsx`, `CourseCard.tsx` |
   507‚Üí| Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `formatDate.ts` |
   508‚Üí| API Routes | kebab-case | `/v1/golf-rounds` |
   509‚Üí| CSS Files | kebab-case | `button-styles.css` |
   510‚Üí| Assets | kebab-case | `og-image.jpg`, `logo-dark.png` |
   511‚Üí
   512‚Üí---
   513‚Üí
   514‚Üí## Folder-Specific CLAUDE.md Files
   515‚Üí
   516‚ÜíAdditional context is loaded automatically when working in these directories:
   517‚Üí
   518‚Üí| Directory | Additional Context |
   519‚Üí|-----------|-------------------|
   520‚Üí| `lzg.home/src/components/` | Component patterns, shadcn rules, styling |
   521‚Üí| `lzg.backend/src/auth/` | Auth module patterns, security guards, CSRF |
   522‚Üí
   523‚Üí---
   524‚Üí
   525‚Üí*For detailed project-specific guidance, see the CLAUDE.md files in each subdirectory.*
   526‚Üí

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Quick Reference](#quick-reference) - Commands, endpoints, test snippets
2. [Repository Structure](#repository-structure) - Directory layout, documentation hierarchy
3. [Development Setup](#development-setup) - Environment, first-time setup
4. [Critical Development Priorities](#-critical-development-priorities) - **READ THIS FIRST**
5. [Golden Rules](#golden-rules-never-break) - Non-negotiable practices
6. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview) - Data flow, modules, models
7. [Environment Variables](#environment-variables) - Configuration
8. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting-quick-reference) - Common issues
9. [Documentation Links](#documentation-links) - All docs in one place
10. [AI Working Principles](#ai-working-principles) - How Claude should work
11. [Brand Guidelines](#brand-guidelines) - Voice, colors, theme
12. [Workspace Organization](#workspace-organization-rules) - Standards

---

## Auto-Discovered Context

Claude Code automatically loads CLAUDE.md files based on working directory:

| Working In | CLAUDE.md Files Loaded |
|------------|----------------------|
| `/` (root) | This file only |
| `/lzg.home/` | This file + `lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` |
| `/lzg.home/src/components/` | This file + `lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` + `components/CLAUDE.md` |
| `/lzg.backend/` | This file + `lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` |
| `/lzg.backend/src/auth/` | This file + `lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` + `auth/CLAUDE.md` |

**Detailed guides imported as needed:**
- Completion checklists: `@docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md`
- Naming conventions: `@docs/guidelines/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md`

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV ‚Üí JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | None | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| `POST /auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |
| `GET /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Get user profile |
| `PUT /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Update profile |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | List rounds (paginated) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | Create round |
| `GET /v1/courses/search` | None | Search courses |

See [API Reference](./docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) for complete endpoint documentation.

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/

## Development Setup

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production Frontend:** https://lazygolfing.com

### First-Time Setup

```bash
# Frontend
cd lzg.home && npm ci && npm run build:data

# Backend (on VM)
cd lzg.backend && npm ci
cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment
npx prisma generate
npx prisma migrate dev
npm run start:dev
```

## ‚ö†ÔłŹ Critical Development Priorities

> **These principles are NON-NEGOTIABLE.** Every decision, feature, and line of code must align with these priorities. When in conflict, priorities higher in the list take precedence.

### Priority Hierarchy (In Order)

#### 1. ūüĒí SECURITY (Non-Negotiable)
- **Defense in depth** - Multiple layers of protection, never rely on single control
- **Never trust user input** - Validate, sanitize, escape at every boundary
- **Secure by default** - New features must be secure out of the box
- **Zero tolerance for secrets in code** - No API keys, tokens, passwords in source
- **OWASP Top 10 awareness** - Actively prevent XSS, injection, CSRF, auth issues
- **Data minimization** - Only collect/store what's absolutely necessary
- **Audit logging** - Track security-relevant actions for forensics

#### 2. ūüŹóÔłŹ FOUNDATIONAL (Build Right)
- **Solid architecture first** - No shortcuts that create tech debt
- **Single source of truth** - One authoritative location for every piece of data
- **Clean integration** - New features must integrate with existing systems cleanly
- **Backward compatibility** - Don't break existing functionality without migration path
- **Database integrity** - Proper constraints, indexes, foreign keys, cascades

#### 3. ūüõ°ÔłŹ STABILITY (Never Crash)
- **Zero tolerance for crashes** - Comprehensive error handling everywhere
- **No data loss** - Every user action either succeeds completely or fails safely
- **Graceful degradation** - Features fail gracefully when dependencies unavailable
- **Test critical paths** - Auth, payments, data mutations must have test coverage
- **Defensive programming** - Assume inputs are malformed, APIs are down, state is corrupted

#### 4. ‚ú® UX/UI (User Delight)
- **Speed is a feature** - Target <100ms perceived interaction latency
- **Mobile-first responsive** - Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
- **Intuitive flows** - Users should never wonder "what do I do next?"
- **Accessibility required** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard nav, screen readers
- **Consistent design language** - Miami Vice theme applied uniformly
- **Feedback on every action** - Loading states, success messages, error explanations
- **Forgiveness** - Easy undo, confirmation for destructive actions

#### 5. ūüéĮ LEAN (Ship Minimal)
- **MVP first** - Ship the minimum viable solution, iterate based on feedback
- **No feature creep** - If it's not solving a real user problem, don't build it
- **Question every dependency** - Each package adds risk, size, and maintenance burden
- **Simpler is better** - Fewer abstractions, less code, easier maintenance
- **Delete before adding** - Remove unused code before adding new features

#### 6. ūüßĻ CLEAN (Readable Code)
- **Readable > clever** - Anyone should understand code without deep context
- **Consistent naming** - Follow established conventions throughout
- **Single responsibility** - Each function/component does one thing well
- **No dead code** - Remove unused imports, functions, files immediately
- **Self-documenting** - Code explains "what", comments explain "why"

#### 7. ūüďą RETENTION (Bring Users Back)
- **Valuable data** - Help users track stats they can't get elsewhere
- **Progress visualization** - Show improvement over time
- **Social features** - Playing partners, comparisons, friendly competition
- **Low friction** - One-tap round logging, smart defaults, remember preferences
- **Notifications done right** - Valuable reminders, never spam

#### 8. ‚ö° EFFICIENT + OPTIMIZED (Performance)
- **Measure first** - Profile before optimizing, no premature optimization
- **Performance budgets** - Set and enforce bundle size limits
- **Core Web Vitals** - LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
- **Database optimization** - Proper indexes, query analysis, N+1 prevention
- **Caching strategy** - Cache at every layer (CDN, API, database, client)
- **Lazy loading** - Load code and data only when needed

#### 9. ūüĒĄ UPDATED + KNOWLEDGEABLE (Stay Current)
- **Current dependencies** - Regular updates, security patches applied promptly
- **Modern patterns** - Use current best practices, avoid deprecated approaches
- **Understand before changing** - Read existing code thoroughly before modifying
- **Know the "why"** - Understand reasoning behind architectural decisions
- **Documentation current** - CLAUDE.md and docs updated with every change

#### 10. ūüŹĆÔłŹ INDUSTRY-FAMILIAR (Golf Domain Expertise)
- **Handicap systems** - GHIN, WHS, course/slope ratings, handicap index calculation
- **Golf terminology** - Correct use of GIR, FIR, putts per round, scoring terms
- **Course data** - Par, rating, slope, tee boxes, layouts
- **Tournament formats** - Stroke play, match play, Stableford, scramble
- **Competitor awareness** - Know what GHIN, Golf Pad, 18Birdies offer

---

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`**
4. **Admin routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`**
5. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
6. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens
7. **Test before deploy** - No untested code reaches production
8. **Mobile-first** - Every feature must work on mobile devices

### Before Completing ANY Task

**Quick checklist** (see `@docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):

- [ ] **Security:** No vulnerabilities (injection, XSS, auth bypass, secrets)
- [ ] **Stability:** Error handling for all failure modes
- [ ] **UX:** Works on mobile, accessible, has loading/error states
- [ ] **Lean:** No unnecessary code, dependencies, or features
- [ ] **Clean:** Follows existing patterns, readable, no dead code
- [ ] **Tests:** `npm run test` (backend) / `npm run build` (frontend) pass
- [ ] **Docs:** CLAUDE.md updated if patterns changed

### CSV Data Workflow

```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
git commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
```

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend Data Flow

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ courses/        # Course management
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ prisma/         # Database service (global module)
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend ‚Üí `GET /auth/google` ‚Üí Google consent
2. Google ‚Üí Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

## Troubleshooting Quick Reference

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

## Workspace Organization Rules

These rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.

### Single Source of Truth Principle

Every piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:

| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |

### Static Asset Organization

All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:

```
public/
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicons/     # All favicon files
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
```

**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).

### Social Sharing Standards

| Standard | Specification |
|----------|--------------|
| OG Image Size | 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio) |
| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |

    42‚Üí## Quick start: viewing the site
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíThe production site is available at [https://tokerleague.com](https://tokerleague.com). To browse a local copy:
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí1. Install Node.js 18 or newer (matches the `engines` field in `package.json`).
    47‚Üí2. Install dependencies and launch any static file server. Two easy options:
    48‚Üí   * **Ad-hoc using npm** ‚Äď `npx http-server .` from the repository root.
    49‚Üí   * **VS¬†Code Live Server** ‚Äď open the folder in VS¬†Code and use the built-in live preview.
    50‚Üí3. Visit `http://localhost:8080/index.html` (or whatever port your server prints). All data loads from the JSON exports under `assets/statistics`, so no additional build step is required.
    51‚Üí
    52‚ÜíBecause every page is static HTML, you can also open the files directly in a browser without a server, but some browsers block `fetch` requests from the local filesystem. Using a simple static server guarantees the data requests succeed.
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí## Repository tour
    55‚Üí
    56‚ÜíThe repo blends the modern Codex-built experience with archived legacy pages. Key locations:
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí| Path | Purpose |
    59‚Üí| --- | --- |
    60‚Üí| `index.html`, `styles.css`, `main.js` | The redesigned league hub rendered on tokerleague.com. JavaScript renders seasonal stats, history, and draft data on demand.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L1-L203„ÄĎ„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L1-L358„ÄĎ|
    61‚Üí| `team.html`, `team.js` | Franchise detail pages linked across the site. They compile lifetime records, highlight reels, and draft footprints for a single identity.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L1-L153„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L1-L189„ÄĎ|
    62‚Üí| `admin.html` | Private commissioner tooling (‚ÄúIdentity Lab‚ÄĚ) for reconciling Yahoo aliases and generating manifest snippets.„ÄźF:admin.html‚Ä†L1-L156„ÄĎ|
    63‚Üí| `assets/statistics/` | Cached outputs from the Yahoo Fantasy sync pipeline‚ÄĒper-season JSON, CSV exports, and the manifest that tells the loader where to find each file.„ÄźF:assets/statistics/manifest.json‚Ä†L1-L8„ÄĎ|
    64‚Üí| `assets/statistics/franchises.json` | Canonical franchise definitions (slugs, colors, alias lists) used for identity matching and theming.„ÄźF:assets/statistics/franchises.json‚Ä†L1-L36„ÄĎ|
    65‚Üí| `data.js` | Shared data loader injected on every dynamic page. It wraps Papa Parse for CSVs, caches JSON payloads, aggregates league-wide metrics, and exposes helper utilities on `window.codexData` for the UI scripts.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1-L1306„ÄĎ|
    66‚Üí| `tools/yahoo-sync/` | Node.js scripts that authenticate with Yahoo, download league data, and update the JSON/CSV caches. Includes a detailed README and CLI entry points declared in `package.json`.„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/README.md‚Ä†L1-L64„ÄĎ„ÄźF:package.json‚Ä†L1-L18„ÄĎ|
    67‚Üí| `current.html`, `history.html`, `tokers.html`, etc. | Legacy static pages preserved for nostalgia. They are not linked from the new hub but remain deployable if you need the old layouts.„ÄźF:current.html‚Ä†L1-L74„ÄĎ|
    68‚Üí| `_version1/`, `_version2/`, `new/` | Archived design experiments. Useful as design references but not part of the active build.

Tags above the list note whether playoffs or consolation games occurred during the selected week.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L329-L383„ÄĎ

   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí## Stat caches and data pipeline
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí### Directory layout
   175‚Üí
   176‚ÜíThe Yahoo sync drops structured files into `assets/statistics/<year>/`. Typical contents include:
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí* `team.json`, `matchups.json`, `draft.json`, `metadata.json` ‚Äď Normalized JSON objects consumed directly by `data.js`.
   179‚Üí* `standings.raw.json`, `scoreboard.raw.json`, `draftresults.raw.json` ‚Äď Original Yahoo payloads preserved for debugging or rebuilding the normalizers.
   180‚Üí* `Yahoo-*.csv` files ‚Äď Raw CSV exports (matchups, rosters, draft board, transactions) used for auditing and as a fallback parser if Yahoo changes its API.„ÄźF:assets/statistics/2015/bundle.json‚Ä†L1-L32„ÄĎ
   181‚Üí* `settings.json`, `transactions.json` ‚Äď Additional context such as roster settings, waiver moves, or commissioner notes.
   182‚Üí
   183‚ÜíA top-level `assets/statistics/manifest.json` drives the loader. Each year lists the files available for teams, matchups, draft data, metadata, and any special handling flags (e.g., hidden seasons).„ÄźF:assets/statistics/manifest.json‚Ä†L1-L8„ÄĎ
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí### Manifest-driven loaders
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí`data.js` fetches the manifest once, caches it, then resolves per-season files as needed. Important behaviors:
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí* **Caching** ‚Äď Results are memoized so repeat visits don‚Äôt re-download JSON unless the page refreshes. `resourceCache` and `seasonCache` manage per-file and per-season entries.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1-L63„ÄĎ
   190‚Üí* **Normalization** ‚Äď When reading CSVs or raw Yahoo structures, helper functions parse numbers safely, standardize streaks, and compute derived stats (point differential, playoff record, etc.).„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L64-L245„ÄĎ
   191‚Üí* **Season composition** ‚Äď `loadSeason(year)` merges teams, matchup logs, draft picks, raw source files, and metadata. It also determines whether the season is complete, calculates total/average points, builds highlight stories, and keeps track of the most recent completed week.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L900-L1088„ÄĎ
   192‚Üí* **League aggregates** ‚Äď `buildAggregates(seasons)` folds season summaries into league-wide totals used by the hero stats, dynasty meter, record book, and draft archives.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1089-L1187„ÄĎ
   193‚Üí* **Utility exports** ‚Äď The loader exposes `getSeasonYears`, `getFranchiseBySlug`, `getFranchiseSlug`, `formatRecord`, `recordWinPct`, and more on `window.codexData`, making them reusable across every page script.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1236-L1306„ÄĎ
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí### Franchise identity mapping
   196‚Üí
   197‚ÜíAccurate links between Yahoo teams and long-running franchises depend on `assets/statistics/franchises.json`. Each entry declares:
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí* Stable `id`/`slug` values used in URLs.
   200‚Üí* The latest display name and manager aliases.
   201‚Üí* Known team aliases observed over the years.
   202‚Üí* Optional `primaryColor`/`secondaryColor` for theming.
   203‚Üí* Flags such as `ignore` (exclude from public displays) or `generated` (auto-created until confirmed).„ÄźF:assets/statistics/franchises.json‚Ä†L1-L36„ÄĎ
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí`data.js` registers each alias so that standings, draft tables, and matchup logs can link back to the correct slug even if Yahoo uses a fresh name each season. The Identity Lab automates the maintenance loop by highlighting unresolved aliases and generating boilerplate entries for the manifest.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1188-L1235„ÄĎ„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L721-L907„ÄĎ
   206‚Üí
   207‚Üí## Front-end architecture
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí* `main.js` owns every interactive widget on `index.html`. It wires up dropdowns, renders tables, listens for selection changes, and calls back into `window.codexData` for data. The file also powers the Identity Lab to avoid duplicating loader logic.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L1-L720„ÄĎ
   210‚Üí* `team.js` shares the same loader but focuses on franchise-specific aggregates, highlight generation, and draft history filtering. It gracefully handles missing data and sets document titles dynamically.„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L1-L239„ÄĎ
   211‚Üí* `styles.css` centralizes layout, typography, responsive breakpoints, and utility classes shared across the modern pages (hero shells, cards, tables, highlight chips, etc.). The same stylesheet covers the Identity Lab and team pages for a unified visual system.„ÄźF:styles.css‚Ä†L1-L400„ÄĎ
   212‚Üí* Papa Parse (`cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/papaparse`) is loaded via CDN to parse CSV backups when JSON isn‚Äôt available. `data.js` automatically chooses the appropriate parser based on each manifest entry‚Äôs `format` value.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L25-L37„ÄĎ„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L5-L63„ÄĎ
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí## Syncing with Yahoo Fantasy
   215‚Üí
   216‚ÜíAll live data originates from the scripts under `tools/yahoo-sync/`.
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí1. **Authenticate** using the OAuth helper:
   219‚Üí   ```bash
   220‚Üí   npm install
   221‚Üí   YAHOO_CLIENT_ID=<client-id> YAHOO_CLIENT_SECRET=<client-secret> npm run yahoo:auth
   222‚Üí   ```
   223‚Üí   The script spins up a callback server (default port 8719), guides you through Yahoo‚Äôs consent screen, and writes the resulting tokens to `.yahoo/tokens.json`. Detailed setup instructions‚ÄĒincluding developer app configuration and Codespaces tips‚ÄĒlive in `tools/yahoo-sync/README.md`.
   224‚Üí2. **Configure credentials** by editing `.yahoo/credentials.json` and committing both credential and token files (the repo is private specifically to allow this workflow).„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/README.md‚Ä†L1-L64„ÄĎ
   225‚Üí3. **Sync stats** with `npm run yahoo:sync`. Optional flags allow targeting a single `--season` or a range via `--from`/`--to`. The CLI throttles requests, respects Yahoo rate limits, and updates the manifest when new files are written. The script also supports snapshotting rosters or player pools via environment toggles like `YAHOO_SNAPSHOT_ROSTERS=true`.„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/sync.js‚Ä†L1-L199„ÄĎ
   226‚Üí4. **Review outputs** in `assets/statistics/<year>/`. Fresh runs overwrite JSON, refresh the manifest, and commit back through GitHub Actions if you trigger the workflow in CI.„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/README.md‚Ä†L33-L64„ÄĎ
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí## Local development tips
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí* Run `npm install` once to grab the Yahoo Fantasy SDK and dotenv helper declared in `package.json`. No bundler is required; scripts are plain ES5 modules loaded with `<script defer>` tags.„ÄźF:package.json‚Ä†L1-L18„ÄĎ„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L31-L38„ÄĎ
   231‚Üí* When editing `data.js`, remember it executes in an IIFE and registers helpers on `window.codexData`. Keep additions idempotent and respect the existing caching maps to avoid redundant network requests.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1-L63„ÄĎ„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1236-L1306„ÄĎ
   232‚Üí* If you add new JSON files to `assets/statistics/<year>/`, update `manifest.json` accordingly so the loader knows where to look. Missing manifest entries will cause dropdowns to skip those seasons.
   233‚Üí* For visual tweaks, edit `styles.css`. The design system is built with CSS variables (e.g., `--team-accent`) that team pages override based on franchise colors. Test both the league hub and a franchise page to ensure changes work everywhere.„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L24-L48„ÄĎ
