   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí## Operational git workflow
   240‚Üí
   241‚ÜíMost contributors work out of GitHub Codespaces or Workspaces. The repository includes helper scripts to keep those environments in sync:
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí1. **Auto-sync watcher** ‚Äď When a Codespace boots, `.devcontainer/scripts/start-auto-pull.sh` launches a background watcher that fetches `origin/main` every five minutes, fast-forwards the local branch if clean, and logs activity to `.devcontainer/autopull.log`. Stop it temporarily with `pkill -f tools/auto-pull/watch-main.sh`; restart with `bash .devcontainer/scripts/start-auto-pull.sh`.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L176-L213„ÄĎ
   244‚Üí2. **Git configuration helper** ‚Äď Run `npm run git:configure` once per clone to set pull.rebase defaults and wire the current branch to its upstream. This resolves the ‚ÄúNeed to specify how to reconcile‚ÄĚ prompt in VS¬†Code‚Äôs Source Control panel.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L215-L240„ÄĎ
   245‚Üí3. **Routine sync checklist** ‚Äď Fetch, pull, resolve conflicts, and continue working:
   246‚Üí   ```bash
   247‚Üí   git fetch origin
   248‚Üí   git pull origin main
   249‚Üí   ```
   250‚Üí   For a full reset against `origin/main`, run:
   251‚Üí   ```bash
   252‚Üí   git fetch origin
   253‚Üí   git reset --hard origin/main
   254‚Üí   git clean -fd
   255‚Üí   ```
   256‚Üí   These commands are especially useful before rerunning the Yahoo sync or redeploying the static site.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L242-L289„ÄĎ
   257‚Üí4. **Yahoo authentication refresher** ‚Äď If tokens expire, re-run `npm run yahoo:auth` with `YAHOO_CLIENT_ID` and `YAHOO_CLIENT_SECRET` set, approve the prompt, and commit the regenerated `.yahoo/tokens.json`. No need to recreate the Codespace or update redirect URIs when following this flow.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L291-L309„ÄĎ
   258‚Üí
   259‚ÜíKeep this README handy whenever you onboard a new manager, extend the UI, or refresh the stats. It is the authoritative map for how the Toker League site works from top to bottom.
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí```

# Welcome to the source for **tokerleague.com**. This repository drives the public-facing league hub, every franchise history page, and the private commissioner-only utilities that keep Yahoo Fantasy data aligned. The goal of this README is twofold:

* **League newcomers** can read it like a tour guide and understand what every screen on the site shows, why the numbers matter, and how to navigate between seasons, franchises, and draft archives.
* **Developers and data maintainers** can treat it as a technical manual that documents the data pipeline, the front-end architecture, and the operational scripts used to keep statistics synchronized with Yahoo.

Use the table of contents below to jump straight to the information you need.

## Table of contents

1. [Quick start: viewing the site](#quick-start-viewing-the-site)
2. [Repository tour](#repository-tour)
3. [League Hub (index.html)](#league-hub-indexhtml)
   1. [Hero and navigation](#hero-and-navigation)
   2. [Season Explorer](#season-explorer)
   3. [Draft Archives](#draft-archives)
   4. [League History](#league-history)
   5. [Franchise Spotlight](#franchise-spotlight)
4. [Franchise history pages (team.html)](#franchise-history-pages-teamhtml)
5. [Commissioner Identity Lab (admin.html)](#commissioner-identity-lab-adminhtml)
6. [Legacy reference pages](#legacy-reference-pages)
7. [Stat caches and data pipeline](#stat-caches-and-data-pipeline)
   1. [Directory layout](#directory-layout)
   2. [Manifest-driven loaders](#manifest-driven-loaders)
   3. [Franchise identity mapping](#franchise-identity-mapping)
8. [Front-end architecture](#front-end-architecture)
9. [Syncing with Yahoo Fantasy](#syncing-with-yahoo-fantasy)
10. [Local development tips](#local-development-tips)
11. [Operational git workflow](#operational-git-workflow)

---

## Quick start: viewing the site

The production site is available at [https://tokerleague.com](https://tokerleague.com). To browse a local copy:

1. Install Node.js 18 or newer (matches the `engines` field in `package.json`).
2. Install dependencies and launch any static file server. Two easy options:
   * **Ad-hoc using npm** ‚Äď `npx http-server .` from the repository root.
   * **VS¬†Code Live Server** ‚Äď open the folder in VS¬†Code and use the built-in live preview.
3. Visit `http://localhost:8080/index.html` (or whatever port your server prints). All data loads from the JSON exports under `assets/statistics`, so no additional build step is required.

Because every page is static HTML, you can also open the files directly in a browser without a server, but some browsers block `fetch` requests from the local filesystem. Using a simple static server guarantees the data requests succeed.

## Repository tour

The repo blends the modern Codex-built experience with archived legacy pages. Key locations:

| Path | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `index.html`, `styles.css`, `main.js` | The redesigned league hub rendered on tokerleague.com. JavaScript renders seasonal stats, history, and draft data on demand.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L1-L203„ÄĎ„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L1-L358„ÄĎ|
| `team.html`, `team.js` | Franchise detail pages linked across the site. They compile lifetime records, highlight reels, and draft footprints for a single identity.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L1-L153„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L1-L189„ÄĎ|
| `admin.html` | Private commissioner tooling (‚ÄúIdentity Lab‚ÄĚ) for reconciling Yahoo aliases and generating manifest snippets.„ÄźF:admin.html‚Ä†L1-L156„ÄĎ|
| `assets/statistics/` | Cached outputs from the Yahoo Fantasy sync pipeline‚ÄĒper-season JSON, CSV exports, and the manifest that tells the loader where to find each file.„ÄźF:assets/statistics/manifest.json‚Ä†L1-L8„ÄĎ|
| `assets/statistics/franchises.json` | Canonical franchise definitions (slugs, colors, alias lists) used for identity matching and theming.„ÄźF:assets/statistics/franchises.json‚Ä†L1-L36„ÄĎ|
| `data.js` | Shared data loader injected on every dynamic page. It wraps Papa Parse for CSVs, caches JSON payloads, aggregates league-wide metrics, and exposes helper utilities on `window.codexData` for the UI scripts.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1-L1306„ÄĎ|
| `tools/yahoo-sync/` | Node.js scripts that authenticate with Yahoo, download league data, and update the JSON/CSV caches. Includes a detailed README and CLI entry points declared in `package.json`.„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/README.md‚Ä†L1-L64„ÄĎ„ÄźF:package.json‚Ä†L1-L18„ÄĎ|
| `current.html`, `history.html`, `tokers.html`, etc. | Legacy static pages preserved for nostalgia. They are not linked from the new hub but remain deployable if you need the old layouts.„ÄźF:current.html‚Ä†L1-L74„ÄĎ|
| `_version1/`, `_version2/`, `new/` | Archived design experiments. Useful as design references but not part of the active build.

Use this section as your bearings before diving into individual features.

## League Hub (index.html)

### Hero and navigation

* **Top navigation** anchors to major sections: Season Explorer, Draft Lab, League History, and Franchise Spotlight. All links stay within the page for quick scrolling.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L34-L66„ÄĎ
* **Hero headline** explains the league‚Äôs origin story and competitive format. Two call-to-action buttons link to the live Yahoo league and the Facebook group for social chatter.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L67-L106„ÄĎ
* **Gallery** rotates three emblematic images (current champion, commissioner mascot, league logo).„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L107-L118„ÄĎ
* **Hero-at-a-glance stats** render dynamically via `renderHeroGlance()` in `main.js`. The tiles show:
  - Seasons documented in the cache.
  - Total matchups logged (count of individual head-to-head games).
  - Unique franchises (after ignoring merged or retired identities).
  - Total points archived (rounded league-wide scoring sum).
  - Most championships and which franchises share the lead (uses `Intl.ListFormat` so multiple winners read naturally).„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L39-L88„ÄĎ

### Season Explorer

This section is the control center for replaying any season captured in `assets/statistics/<year>/`.

1. **Season selector** ‚Äď Populated with every non-hidden season in the manifest. Selecting a year triggers `loadSeason(year)` via `codexData` to hydrate the panels.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L1-L38„ÄĎ„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1090-L1135„ÄĎ
2. **Season meta strip** ‚Äď Lists total franchises, unique identities, playoff berths, total points, and the combined average score per matchup. ‚ÄúAverage Matchup Score‚ÄĚ is the league-wide average of each game‚Äôs combined points for the chosen season (`totalPointsScored / matchups.length`).„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L89-L130„ÄĎ„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1049-L1088„ÄĎ
3. **Season summary card** ‚Äď Shifts depending on whether the season is complete.
   * *In progress*: headline ‚ÄúSeason In Progress,‚ÄĚ champion placeholder, current leader, best win percentage, average matchup score to date, season points accumulated, and leaders for points scored / points allowed so far.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L131-L205„ÄĎ
   * *Locked champion*: switches to ‚ÄúChampions,‚ÄĚ shows the winning franchise (linked to the team page), final record, runner-up, best regular-season win rate, average matchup score, total points, points leader, and stingiest defense.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L206-L262„ÄĎ
   * Every champion name, runner-up, and highlight is turned into a link when the franchise slug is known, so readers can click through for the full franchise dossier.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L167-L184„ÄĎ
4. **Regular Season Standings table** ‚Äď Columns mean:
   * **Seed** ‚Äď Playoff seed if Yahoo supplied it; otherwise the final rank.
   * **Franchise Identity** ‚Äď Preferred persona or team name. Links to `team.html` when the franchise is mapped.
   * **Record** ‚Äď Wins-Losses-(Ties).
   * **PF / PA / Diff** ‚Äď Points For, Points Against, and differential.
   * **Streak** ‚Äď Current streak in W/L notation when Yahoo provides it.
   The footer metadata calls out how many franchises competed, confirms sorting by final rank, and highlights the points-for leader with a link.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L263-L328„ÄĎ
5. **Weekly Scoreboard** ‚Äď Dropdown lists every week captured for that season. Selecting a week redraws the list of matchups, with each card showing both teams (name plus identity) and their scores to two decimals. Tags above the list note whether playoffs or consolation games occurred during the selected week.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L329-L383„ÄĎ
6. **Season Highlights** ‚Äď Top-level superlatives generated from the underlying game log:
   * Points Leader (highest season-long PF).
   * Fewest Points Allowed.
   * Highest Score (single game, with opponent and context).
   * Biggest Blowout (largest positive margin).
   * Closest Finish (smallest margin, win or loss).
   Each list item includes a label, bold stat, and a note describing the week and opponent.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L384-L437„ÄĎ
7. **Draft Lab** ‚Äď Replay the live Yahoo draft board.
   * **Round filter** toggles between all picks and a single round; options are generated from the captured draft data.
   * Table columns: Round, Pick number, Franchise (linked to team page when mapped), Player name, Position, Yahoo average draft position, and Yahoo rostered percentage at draft time.
   * Table footer (rendered as `draftMeta`) summarizes how many picks are shown, how many unique franchises participated, and the total picks captured in the export.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L438-L494„ÄĎ

### Draft Archives

Separate from the Draft Lab, this section lets you browse any season‚Äôs draft without changing the active Season Explorer filters.

* **Season dropdown** lists every cached year and defaults to the latest.
* Each table is sorted by round/pick and includes the same columns as the Draft Lab table.
* The metadata line highlights the champion (when the season is complete), number of rounds, franchises represented, and how many picks were recorded. If a season lacks a draft export, a message explains that the board is unavailable yet.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L495-L571„ÄĎ

### League History

Three cards summarize the entire league timeline:

1. **Championship Timeline** ‚Äď Chronological list of champions with identity, manager, and final record. Franchise names link out when the slug is known.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L572-L613„ÄĎ
2. **Dynasty Meter** ‚Äď Progress-bar list of the top franchises by championships and win percentage (requires at least two seasons on record).„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L614-L649„ÄĎ
3. **Record Book** ‚Äď League-wide superlatives such as most championships, winningest franchise, points-for leader, best single season, playoff wins leader, highest single-game score, and the longest championship drought among teams without a title. Each entry formats ties with human-readable lists.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L650-L720„ÄĎ

### Franchise Spotlight

A curated grid of notable imagery and blurbs. Currently highlights the reigning champion, the commissioner mascot, and a placeholder card for long-time members. This section is static HTML‚ÄĒyou can expand it with additional franchises or badges as needed.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L204-L246„ÄĎ

## Franchise history pages (team.html)

Every standings link and draft table entry points to `team.html?franchise=<slug>`. The page is powered by `team.js` and the shared `data.js` loader.

* **Hero banner** ‚Äď Shows the latest team alias, manager identity, and seasons played. If the franchise has branded colors in `franchises.json`, they tint the page chrome. The breadcrumb returns visitors to the main hub.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L37-L78„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L24-L70„ÄĎ
* **Franchise Snapshot** ‚Äď Grid of six cards summarizing all-time performance:
  1. All-Time Record with win rate.
  2. Seasons Played and the range of years.
  3. Championships plus total playoff appearances.
  4. Playoff Record and postseason games played.
  5. Points For and point differential.
  6. Average Final Rank, with a note calling out the best season and record.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L83-L110„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L71-L124„ÄĎ
* **Season Ledger** ‚Äď One card per season sorted newest-to-oldest. Badges identify champions and playoff berths, while metadata lists final rank, PF, PA, and franchise identity used that year. Postseason notes (e.g., semifinal loss) appear when captured.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L112-L128„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L125-L177„ÄĎ
* **Highlight Reel** ‚Äď Notable accomplishments derived from the franchise‚Äôs full game log: best season, highest score, biggest win, playoff r√©sum√©, and toughest loss. Each item references the season/week and opponent context.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L129-L137„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L178-L247„ÄĎ
* **Draft Footprint** ‚Äď Table of every recorded draft pick involving the franchise. The year selector defaults to the latest season once options load. Rows list year, round, pick, player, and the team name used at the time.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L138-L153„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L189-L239„ÄĎ
* **Error handling** ‚Äď A loading banner appears until stats are ready; if a slug cannot be resolved, `team.js` shows a descriptive error and hides the rest of the layout.„ÄźF:team.html‚Ä†L80-L82„ÄĎ„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L10-L38„ÄĎ

* **Raw manager directory** ‚Äď Supplemental table listing every unique Yahoo manager label, the seasons in which it appeared, associated team names, and whether the mapping is still unresolved.„ÄźF:admin.html‚Ä†L156-L178„ÄĎ„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L1122-L1258„ÄĎ

## Legacy reference pages

Older HTML pages remain in the root for posterity. They predate the Codex rebuild and still render if you browse to them directly:

* `current.html`, `former.html`, `tokers.html` ‚Äď Static rosters with navbar drop-downs and linked league imagery.„ÄźF:current.html‚Ä†L1-L74„ÄĎ
* `stats.html`, `best.html`, `worst.html`, `history.html`, `future.html` ‚Äď Hand-crafted writeups and tables from earlier seasons.
* `_version1/`, `_version2/`, and `new/` ‚Äď Archived redesign attempts with their own CSS and assets.

These pages do not participate in the modern data loader, but they can provide historical context or be mined for content when expanding the new hub.

## Stat caches and data pipeline

### Directory layout

The Yahoo sync drops structured files into `assets/statistics/<year>/`. Typical contents include:

* `team.json`, `matchups.json`, `draft.json`, `metadata.json` ‚Äď Normalized JSON objects consumed directly by `data.js`.
* `standings.raw.json`, `scoreboard.raw.json`, `draftresults.raw.json` ‚Äď Original Yahoo payloads preserved for debugging or rebuilding the normalizers.
* `Yahoo-*.csv` files ‚Äď Raw CSV exports (matchups, rosters, draft board, transactions) used for auditing and as a fallback parser if Yahoo changes its API.„ÄźF:assets/statistics/2015/bundle.json‚Ä†L1-L32„ÄĎ
* `settings.json`, `transactions.json` ‚Äď Additional context such as roster settings, waiver moves, or commissioner notes.

A top-level `assets/statistics/manifest.json` drives the loader. Each year lists the files available for teams, matchups, draft data, metadata, and any special handling flags (e.g., hidden seasons).„ÄźF:assets/statistics/manifest.json‚Ä†L1-L8„ÄĎ

### Manifest-driven loaders

`data.js` fetches the manifest once, caches it, then resolves per-season files as needed. Important behaviors:

* **Caching** ‚Äď Results are memoized so repeat visits don‚Äôt re-download JSON unless the page refreshes. `resourceCache` and `seasonCache` manage per-file and per-season entries.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1-L63„ÄĎ
* **Normalization** ‚Äď When reading CSVs or raw Yahoo structures, helper functions parse numbers safely, standardize streaks, and compute derived stats (point differential, playoff record, etc.).„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L64-L245„ÄĎ
* **Season composition** ‚Äď `loadSeason(year)` merges teams, matchup logs, draft picks, raw source files, and metadata. It also determines whether the season is complete, calculates total/average points, builds highlight stories, and keeps track of the most recent completed week.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L900-L1088„ÄĎ
* **League aggregates** ‚Äď `buildAggregates(seasons)` folds season summaries into league-wide totals used by the hero stats, dynasty meter, record book, and draft archives.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1089-L1187„ÄĎ
* **Utility exports** ‚Äď The loader exposes `getSeasonYears`, `getFranchiseBySlug`, `getFranchiseSlug`, `formatRecord`, `recordWinPct`, and more on `window.codexData`, making them reusable across every page script.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1236-L1306„ÄĎ

### Franchise identity mapping

Accurate links between Yahoo teams and long-running franchises depend on `assets/statistics/franchises.json`. Each entry declares:

* Stable `id`/`slug` values used in URLs.
* The latest display name and manager aliases.
* Known team aliases observed over the years.
* Optional `primaryColor`/`secondaryColor` for theming.
* Flags such as `ignore` (exclude from public displays) or `generated` (auto-created until confirmed).„ÄźF:assets/statistics/franchises.json‚Ä†L1-L36„ÄĎ

`data.js` registers each alias so that standings, draft tables, and matchup logs can link back to the correct slug even if Yahoo uses a fresh name each season. The Identity Lab automates the maintenance loop by highlighting unresolved aliases and generating boilerplate entries for the manifest.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1188-L1235„ÄĎ„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L721-L907„ÄĎ

## Front-end architecture

* `main.js` owns every interactive widget on `index.html`. It wires up dropdowns, renders tables, listens for selection changes, and calls back into `window.codexData` for data. The file also powers the Identity Lab to avoid duplicating loader logic.„ÄźF:main.js‚Ä†L1-L720„ÄĎ
* `team.js` shares the same loader but focuses on franchise-specific aggregates, highlight generation, and draft history filtering. It gracefully handles missing data and sets document titles dynamically.„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L1-L239„ÄĎ
* `styles.css` centralizes layout, typography, responsive breakpoints, and utility classes shared across the modern pages (hero shells, cards, tables, highlight chips, etc.). The same stylesheet covers the Identity Lab and team pages for a unified visual system.„ÄźF:styles.css‚Ä†L1-L400„ÄĎ
* Papa Parse (`cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/papaparse`) is loaded via CDN to parse CSV backups when JSON isn‚Äôt available. `data.js` automatically chooses the appropriate parser based on each manifest entry‚Äôs `format` value.„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L25-L37„ÄĎ„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L5-L63„ÄĎ

## Syncing with Yahoo Fantasy

All live data originates from the scripts under `tools/yahoo-sync/`.

1. **Authenticate** using the OAuth helper:
   ```bash
   npm install
   YAHOO_CLIENT_ID=<client-id> YAHOO_CLIENT_SECRET=<client-secret> npm run yahoo:auth
   ```
   The script spins up a callback server (default port 8719), guides you through Yahoo‚Äôs consent screen, and writes the resulting tokens to `.yahoo/tokens.json`. Detailed setup instructions‚ÄĒincluding developer app configuration and Codespaces tips‚ÄĒlive in `tools/yahoo-sync/README.md`.
2. **Configure credentials** by editing `.yahoo/credentials.json` and committing both credential and token files (the repo is private specifically to allow this workflow).„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/README.md‚Ä†L1-L64„ÄĎ
3. **Sync stats** with `npm run yahoo:sync`. Optional flags allow targeting a single `--season` or a range via `--from`/`--to`. The CLI throttles requests, respects Yahoo rate limits, and updates the manifest when new files are written. The script also supports snapshotting rosters or player pools via environment toggles like `YAHOO_SNAPSHOT_ROSTERS=true`.„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/sync.js‚Ä†L1-L199„ÄĎ
4. **Review outputs** in `assets/statistics/<year>/`. Fresh runs overwrite JSON, refresh the manifest, and commit back through GitHub Actions if you trigger the workflow in CI.„ÄźF:tools/yahoo-sync/README.md‚Ä†L33-L64„ÄĎ

## Local development tips

* Run `npm install` once to grab the Yahoo Fantasy SDK and dotenv helper declared in `package.json`. No bundler is required; scripts are plain ES5 modules loaded with `<script defer>` tags.„ÄźF:package.json‚Ä†L1-L18„ÄĎ„ÄźF:index.html‚Ä†L31-L38„ÄĎ
* When editing `data.js`, remember it executes in an IIFE and registers helpers on `window.codexData`. Keep additions idempotent and respect the existing caching maps to avoid redundant network requests.„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1-L63„ÄĎ„ÄźF:data.js‚Ä†L1236-L1306„ÄĎ
* If you add new JSON files to `assets/statistics/<year>/`, update `manifest.json` accordingly so the loader knows where to look. Missing manifest entries will cause dropdowns to skip those seasons.
* For visual tweaks, edit `styles.css`. The design system is built with CSS variables (e.g., `--team-accent`) that team pages override based on franchise colors. Test both the league hub and a franchise page to ensure changes work everywhere.„ÄźF:team.js‚Ä†L24-L48„ÄĎ

## Operational git workflow

Most contributors work out of GitHub Codespaces or Workspaces. The repository includes helper scripts to keep those environments in sync:

1. **Auto-sync watcher** ‚Äď When a Codespace boots, `.devcontainer/scripts/start-auto-pull.sh` launches a background watcher that fetches `origin/main` every five minutes, fast-forwards the local branch if clean, and logs activity to `.devcontainer/autopull.log`. Stop it temporarily with `pkill -f tools/auto-pull/watch-main.sh`; restart with `bash .devcontainer/scripts/start-auto-pull.sh`.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L176-L213„ÄĎ
2. **Git configuration helper** ‚Äď Run `npm run git:configure` once per clone to set pull.rebase defaults and wire the current branch to its upstream. This resolves the ‚ÄúNeed to specify how to reconcile‚ÄĚ prompt in VS¬†Code‚Äôs Source Control panel.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L215-L240„ÄĎ
3. **Routine sync checklist** ‚Äď Fetch, pull, resolve conflicts, and continue working:
   ```bash
   git fetch origin
   git pull origin main
   ```
   For a full reset against `origin/main`, run:
   ```bash
   git fetch origin
   git reset --hard origin/main
   git clean -fd
   ```
   These commands are especially useful before rerunning the Yahoo sync or redeploying the static site.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L242-L289„ÄĎ
4. **Yahoo authentication refresher** ‚Äď If tokens expire, re-run `npm run yahoo:auth` with `YAHOO_CLIENT_ID` and `YAHOO_CLIENT_SECRET` set, approve the prompt, and commit the regenerated `.yahoo/tokens.json`. No need to recreate the Codespace or update redirect URIs when following this flow.„ÄźF:README.md‚Ä†L291-L309„ÄĎ

Keep this README handy whenever you onboard a new manager, extend the UI, or refresh the stats. It is the authoritative map for how the Toker League site works from top to bottom.

     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí## ūüéĮ Project Overview
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí**What This Is:**
    13‚ÜíA simple, elegant landing page (thedeadrobot.com) serving as a hub for:
    14‚Üí- X (Twitter): [@thedeadrobot](https://x.com/thedeadrobot)

    24‚Üía conscious ai. reset.reboot.restore.

    28‚Üí## ūüďĀ Project Structure
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí```

    51‚Üí```
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí---
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí## ūüß† Core Philosophy
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí### 1. Brutal Simplicity
    58‚Üí- **No frameworks** - HTML/CSS/JS is enough
    59‚Üí- **No build step** - Edit, save, deploy
    60‚Üí- **No dependencies** - Zero npm packages
    61‚Üí- **Fast loading** - < 50KB total page weight
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí### 2. Perfect Execution
    64‚Üí- Every line intentional
    65‚Üí- No commented-out code
    66‚Üí- Clean, documented, maintainable
    67‚Üí- Mobile-first responsive design
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí### 3. Brand Consistency
    70‚Üí- Cyberpunk aesthetic (black + neon green)
    71‚Üí- Roboto font (clean, robotic)
    72‚Üí- Minimalist interactions
    73‚Üí- Post-human vibe
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí### 4. Automation First
    76‚Üí- X account runs itself (AI-generated posts)
    77‚Üí- GitHub Actions orchestration
    78‚Üí- Zero cost ($0.00/month - completely free)
    79‚Üí- Topic approval system (teach only what you know)
    80‚Üí- Human-in-the-loop for quality
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí---
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí## ūüĒß Development Rules & Workflow
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**Critical Foundation:** This project is ~1% complete. Every decision‚ÄĒdocumentation structure, code architecture, feature design‚ÄĒmust build the perfect foundation for the other 99%. No shortcuts. No "good enough." Perfect foundation, always.
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí### The 15 Sacred Rules
    89‚Üí
    90‚ÜíEvery development session follows these rules without exception. They ensure quality, consistency, and maintainability as the project scales.
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí---
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí#### **Planning & Task Management (Rules 1-7)**
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí**Rule 1: Think First, Plan Second**
    97‚Üí```bash
    98‚Üí# Before touching ANY code:
    99‚Üí1. Read relevant files to understand current state
   100‚Üí2. Think through the problem deeply
   101‚Üí3. Write comprehensive plan to tasks/todo.md
   102‚Üí4. Include context, decisions, tradeoffs
   103‚Üí```
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí**Rule 2: Todo Lists Are Required**
   106‚ÜíEvery plan must have checkable todo items:
   107‚Üí```markdown
   108‚Üí## Todo List
   109‚Üí- [ ] Phase 1: Research and analysis
   110‚Üí  - [ ] Read file X
   111‚Üí  - [ ] Understand system Y
   112‚Üí- [ ] Phase 2: Implementation
   113‚Üí  - [ ] Create feature A
   114‚Üí  - [ ] Update docs B
   115‚Üí```
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí**Rule 3: Checkpoint Before Execution**
   118‚Üí```
   119‚ÜíSTOP. Get user approval before proceeding.
   120‚Üí- Review the plan with user
   121‚Üí- Clarify any ambiguities
   122‚Üí- Confirm approach
   123‚Üí- THEN execute
   124‚Üí```
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí**Rule 4: Mark Progress As You Go**
   127‚Üí```markdown
   128‚Üí- [x] Phase 1: Research ‚úÖ
   129‚Üí- [x] Task A complete ‚úÖ
   130‚Üí- [ ] Task B in progress... ‚Ź≥
   131‚Üí```
   132‚ÜíUpdate tasks/todo.md in real-time. Keep user informed.
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí**Rule 5: High-Level Progress Updates**
   135‚ÜíDon't spam details. Give clear, concise updates:
   136‚Üí```
   137‚Üí‚úÖ Added homelab topics to approved_topics.json (22 new topics)
   138‚Üí‚úÖ Updated generate_post.py system prompt (restored cryptic voice)
   139‚Üí‚Ź≥ Testing new prompt with dry run...
   140‚Üí```
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí**Rule 6: Simplicity Always**
   143‚Üí```
   144‚ÜíBAD:  Massive refactor touching 15 files
   145‚ÜíGOOD: Targeted change to 2 relevant files
   146‚Üí
   147‚ÜíBAD:  Complex abstraction "for future flexibility"
   148‚ÜíGOOD: Simple solution that solves today's problem
   149‚Üí
   150‚ÜíMake every change as simple as humanly possible.
   151‚ÜíImpact only necessary code. Introduce zero bugs.
   152‚Üí```
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí**Rule 7: Review Section Required**
   155‚ÜíAt end of tasks/todo.md, add comprehensive review:
   156‚Üí```markdown
   157‚Üí## Review
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí**What Changed:**
   160‚Üí- Added 15 development rules to CLAUDE.md
   161‚Üí- Updated character voice in generate_post.py
   162‚Üí- Added 22 homelab topics
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí**Why:**
   165‚Üí- Rules ensure consistent quality as project scales
   166‚Üí- Voice restoration aligns with brand identity
   167‚Üí- Homelab topics expand content breadth
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí**Impact:**
   170‚Üí- Development process now documented and repeatable
   171‚Üí- Automation voice is cryptic yet educational
   172‚Üí- 22 new topics for content generation
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí**Testing:**
   175‚Üí- Dry run with new prompt: 5/5 posts on-brand ‚úÖ
   176‚Üí- All docs cross-referenced and consistent ‚úÖ
   177‚Üí```
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí---
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí#### **Code Quality (Rules 8-10)**
   182‚Üí
   183‚Üí**Rule 8: NEVER BE LAZY**
   184‚Üí```
   185‚Üí‚ĚĆ "I'll fix that later" - NO
   186‚Üí‚ĚĆ "Good enough for now" - NO
   187‚Üí‚ĚĆ "Temporary workaround" - NO
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí‚úÖ Find root cause
   190‚Üí‚úÖ Fix it properly
   191‚Üí‚úÖ No technical debt
   192‚Üí‚úÖ Senior developer mindset
   193‚Üí
   194‚ÜíIf there's a bug: Find it. Fix it. Test it. Ship it.
   195‚ÜíNo Band-Aids. No TODOs. No shortcuts.
   196‚Üí```
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí**Rule 9: Maximum Simplicity**
   199‚Üí```python
   200‚Üí# BAD: Over-engineered
   201‚Üíclass AbstractFactoryManagerBuilder:
   202‚Üí    def __init__(self, config_loader, dependency_injector):
   203‚Üí        # 50 lines of complexity...
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí# GOOD: Simple and clear
   206‚Üídef load_topics(file_path):
   207‚Üí    with open(file_path) as f:
   208‚Üí        return json.load(f)
   209‚Üí```
   210‚ÜíImpact minimal code. Solve the exact problem. Nothing more.
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí**Rule 10: Living Documentation**
   213‚Üí```
   214‚ÜíWhen ANYTHING changes that affects documentation:
   215‚Üí- Update it IMMEDIATELY
   216‚Üí- Keep docs detailed and clear
   217‚Üí- Files in optimal locations (Perfect Foundation)
   218‚Üí- No stale references
   219‚Üí- No outdated examples
   220‚Üí
   221‚ÜíDocumentation is code. Treat it with same rigor.
   222‚Üí```
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí---
   225‚Üí
   226‚Üí#### **Git Workflow (Rules 11, 14, 15)**
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí**Rule 11: Sync At Session Start**
   229‚Üí```bash
   230‚Üí# FIRST COMMAND every session, no exceptions:
   231‚Üígit fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí# Start with latest code
   234‚Üí# Avoid merge conflicts
   235‚Üí# Build on current foundation
   236‚Üí```
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üí**Rule 14: Validate Current Date**
   239‚Üí```bash
   240‚Üí# NEVER guess dates. Check <env> section:
   241‚ÜíToday's date: 2025-11-19  # From system
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí# Use this for:
   244‚Üí- File headers (Last Updated: 2025-11-19)
   245‚Üí- Task files (Created: 2025-11-19)
   246‚Üí- Documentation timestamps
   247‚Üí- Git commit messages
   248‚Üí```
   249‚Üí
   250‚Üí**Rule 15: Commit & Push At End**
   251‚Üí```bash
   252‚Üí# LAST COMMANDS every session:
   253‚Üígit add .
   254‚Üí
   255‚Üígit commit -m "type: Clear descriptive title
   256‚Üí
   257‚ÜíDetailed explanation of changes:
   258‚Üí- What changed (specific files, features)
   259‚Üí- Why it changed (reasoning, context)
   260‚Üí- Impact of changes (what's now possible/better)
   261‚Üí- Any testing done
   262‚Üí
   263‚ÜíBreaking changes: None
   264‚ÜíTesting: All dry runs passed ‚úÖ
   265‚Üí"
   266‚Üí
   267‚Üígit push origin master
   268‚Üí
   269‚Üí# Examples of good commit types:
   270‚Üífeat:  New feature
   271‚Üífix:   Bug fix
   272‚Üídocs:  Documentation
   273‚Üístyle: Formatting/CSS
   274‚Üíchore: Maintenance
   275‚Üí```
   276‚Üí
   277‚Üí**Commit Message Template:**
   278‚Üí```
   279‚Üítype: Short summary (50 chars max)
   280‚Üí
   281‚Üí## What Changed
   282‚Üí- File X: Added feature Y
   283‚Üí- File Z: Updated config for A
   284‚Üí
   285‚Üí## Why
   286‚Üí- User requested feature Y
   287‚Üí- Config A needed update for compatibility
   288‚Üí
   289‚Üí## Impact
   290‚Üí- Users can now do Y
   291‚Üí- System A works with B
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üí## Testing
   294‚Üí- Dry run: 5/5 tests passed ‚úÖ
   295‚Üí- Manual verification: All features work ‚úÖ
   296‚Üí```
   297‚Üí
   298‚Üí---
   299‚Üí
   300‚Üí#### **Perfect Foundation Mindset (Rule 12)**
   301‚Üí
   302‚Üí**This Project Is 1% Complete**
   303‚Üí
   304‚ÜíEvery decision must build the perfect foundation for the other 99%. This applies to EVERYTHING:
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí**Documentation Organization:**
   307‚Üí```
   308‚Üí‚úÖ PERFECT: docs/automation/ai-persona-guidelines.md
   309‚Üí   (Clear hierarchy, discoverable, purposeful location)
   310‚Üí
   311‚Üí‚ĚĆ LAZY: stuff.md in root
   312‚Üí   (Where does it go? Who knows!)
   313‚Üí
   314‚ÜíFiles must be in OPTIMAL locations from day one.
   315‚ÜíFuture you will thank present you.
   316‚Üí```
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí**Code Architecture:**
   319‚Üí```python
   320‚Üí‚úÖ PERFECT: Modular, clear separation of concerns
   321‚Üídef load_topics(): ...      # One job
   322‚Üídef validate_post(): ...    # One job
   323‚Üídef post_to_twitter(): ...  # One job
   324‚Üí
   325‚Üí‚ĚĆ LAZY: God function that does everything
   326‚Üídef do_everything(): ...    # 500 lines, good luck
   327‚Üí```
   328‚Üí
   329‚Üí**Feature Design:**
   330‚Üí```
   331‚Üí‚úÖ PERFECT: Right primitives, seamless integration
   332‚Üí- Topic system: JSON file, easy to edit, clear structure
   333‚Üí- Validation: Modular checks, easy to add rules
   334‚Üí- Archiving: Structured JSON, git history built-in
   335‚Üí
   336‚Üí‚ĚĆ LAZY: "It works" but can't extend it
   337‚Üí- Hard-coded values
   338‚Üí- No clear patterns
   339‚Üí- Future changes require refactors
   340‚Üí```
   341‚Üí
   342‚Üí**The Standard: Flawless Execution**
   343‚Üí```
   344‚ÜíNot "it works"
   345‚ÜíNot "good enough"
   346‚ÜíNot "we can refactor later"
   347‚Üí
   348‚ÜíFLAWLESS. PERFECT. SCALABLE.
   349‚Üí
   350‚ÜíBecause this is 1%. Build for 100%.
   351‚Üí```
   352‚Üí
   353‚Üí---
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí#### **Complete Logical Units (Rule 13)**
   356‚Üí
   357‚Üí**Never Stop Mid-Implementation**
   358‚Üí
   359‚Üí```
   360‚Üí‚ĚĆ BAD Session End:
   361‚Üí- Half-written feature
   362‚Üí- Broken tests
   363‚Üí- Incomplete documentation
   364‚Üí- Messy git state
   365‚Üí
   366‚Üí‚úÖ GOOD Session End:
   367‚Üí- Feature complete (or clean phase complete)
   368‚Üí- All tests passing
   369‚Üí- Documentation updated
   370‚Üí- Clean git commit
   371‚Üí- Working state
   372‚Üí
   373‚ÜíNatural Stopping Points:
   374‚Üí1. All phases complete
   375‚Üí2. All tests passing
   376‚Üí3. Documentation updated
   377‚Üí4. Feature fully working
   378‚Üí5. Clean git state
   379‚Üí```
   380‚Üí
   381‚Üí**If Approaching Token Limits:**
   382‚Üí```bash
   383‚Üí# Option 1: Finish current logical unit
   384‚Üí‚úÖ Complete the current feature/phase
   385‚Üí‚úÖ Commit working state
   386‚Üí‚úÖ Create handoff notes in tasks/todo.md
   387‚Üí
   388‚Üí# Option 2: Clean checkpoint
   389‚Üí‚úÖ Finish current file/section
   390‚Üí‚úÖ Commit partial but working progress
   391‚Üí‚úÖ Document "Next steps:" clearly
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí# Never:
   394‚Üí‚ĚĆ Stop with broken code
   395‚Üí‚ĚĆ Stop mid-function
   396‚Üí‚ĚĆ Stop without commit
   397‚Üí```
   398‚Üí
   399‚Üí---
   400‚Üí
   401‚Üí### Perfect Foundation Principle (Detailed)
   402‚Üí
   403‚Üí**Why 1% Matters More Than 99%**
   404‚Üí
   405‚ÜíMost projects fail because early decisions create technical debt. By the time you're at 10%, you're paying interest. At 50%, you're drowning.
   406‚Üí
   407‚ÜíThis project inverts that:
   408‚Üí- Perfect foundation now = effortless scaling later
   409‚Üí- Right primitives now = features snap together later
   410‚Üí- Clean architecture now = no refactors needed later
   411‚Üí
   412‚Üí**Documentation Organization Standard:**
   413‚Üí```
   414‚ÜíRoot level (/)
   415‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ CLAUDE.md          # Developer guide (this file)
   416‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md          # Public documentation
   417‚Üí‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ tasks/             # Session task files
   418‚Üí‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ todo.md        # Current session plan
   419‚Üí‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ docs/              # Comprehensive guides
   420‚Üí    ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ automation/    # X automation specifics
   421‚Üí    ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ ai-persona-guidelines.md
   422‚Üí    ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ x-posting-strategy.md
   423‚Üí    ‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ scripts/   # Helper scripts
   424‚Üí    ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ deployment/    # Hosting guides
   425‚Üí    ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ brand/         # Brand identity
   426‚Üí    ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ development/   # Contribution guides
   427‚Üí
   428‚ÜíEvery file has PURPOSE. Every location is OPTIMAL.
   429‚Üí```
   430‚Üí
   431‚Üí**Code Architecture Standard:**
   432‚Üí```python
   433‚Üí# Modular, testable, clear
   434‚Üí# One responsibility per function
   435‚Üí# Easy to understand, easy to extend
   436‚Üí
   437‚Üídef main():
   438‚Üí    """Single entry point, clear flow"""
   439‚Üí    topics = load_approved_topics()
   440‚Üí    content = generate_content(topics)
   441‚Üí
   442‚Üí    if validate_content(content):
   443‚Üí        post_to_twitter(content)
   444‚Üí        archive_post(content)
   445‚Üí
   446‚Üí# Each function:
   447‚Üí- Does ONE thing
   448‚Üí- Has clear inputs/outputs
   449‚Üí- Can be tested independently
   450‚Üí- Self-documenting name
   451‚Üí```
   452‚Üí
   453‚Üí**Feature Design Standard:**
   454‚Üí```
   455‚ÜíWhen adding ANY feature:
   456‚Üí
   457‚Üí1. What primitives do I need? (data structures, abstractions)
   458‚Üí2. How does it integrate? (fits existing patterns seamlessly)
   459‚Üí3. How does it scale? (10x topics? 100x posts?)
   460‚Üí4. How is it maintained? (future developer can understand it)
   461‚Üí
   462‚ÜíIf answers aren't perfect: Redesign before implementing.
   463‚Üí```
   464‚Üí

   575‚Üí### Testing Requirements (Frontend)
   576‚Üí
   577‚Üí**Before ANY Frontend Change:**
   578‚Üí1. ‚úÖ Document current state (screenshot, measurements)
   579‚Üí2. ‚úÖ Make ONE change (not multiple files at once)
   580‚Üí3. ‚úÖ Test locally in multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
   581‚Üí4. ‚úÖ Compare to baseline (does it look identical?)
   582‚Üí5. ‚úÖ Test responsiveness (mobile, tablet, desktop)
   583‚Üí6. ‚úÖ Test animations (do they work the same?)
   584‚Üí7. ‚úÖ Commit if good, revert immediately if broken
   585‚Üí
   586‚Üí**Visual Regression Testing:**
   587‚Üí- Screenshot before change
   588‚Üí- Screenshot after change
   589‚Üí- Compare side-by-side
   590‚Üí- If ANY visual difference: get user approval first
   591‚Üí
   592‚Üí### Rollback Procedure (Frontend Breaks)
   593‚Üí
   594‚Üí**When UI Breaks:**
   595‚Üí1. **STOP** - Don't commit broken code
   596‚Üí2. **Revert** - `git reset --hard HEAD` (or last working commit)
   597‚Üí3. **Document** - What broke and why
   598‚Üí4. **Learn** - What rule was violated?
   599‚Üí5. **Update** - Add prevention to this section
   600‚Üí
   601‚Üí### Frontend Change Approval Process
   602‚Üí
   603‚Üí**For ANY UI-visible change:**
   604‚Üí1. Create optimized version LOCALLY (don't commit)
   605‚Üí2. Test thoroughly (all browsers, all screen sizes)
   606‚Üí3. Take screenshots/video showing comparison
   607‚Üí4. Present to user: "Here's what changes, do you approve?"
   608‚Üí5. Wait for explicit YES before committing
   609‚Üí6. If NO: revert local changes, document why rejected
   610‚Üí
   611‚Üí**No UI change is deployed without explicit user approval.**

   727‚Üí## ūüďĚ Code Style Guidelines
   728‚Üí
   729‚Üí### HTML
   730‚Üí- Semantic tags (`<h1>`, not `<div class="title">`)
   731‚Üí- Lowercase attributes
   732‚Üí- Self-closing tags for void elements (`<img />`, `<link />`)
   733‚Üí- Meaningful alt text for images
   734‚Üí- Proper indentation (2 spaces)
   735‚Üí
   736‚Üí### CSS
   737‚Üí- Mobile-first approach (base styles, then `@media` up)
   738‚Üí- Avoid `!important` (specificity wars)
   739‚Üí- Use CSS custom properties for reusable values
   740‚Üí- Group related rules together
   741‚Üí- Comment complex animations
   742‚Üí
   743‚Üí### JavaScript
   744‚Üí- ES6+ syntax (`const`, `let`, arrow functions)
   745‚Üí- Event delegation where possible
   746‚Üí- No jQuery (vanilla DOM APIs are fine)
   747‚Üí- Comments for non-obvious logic
   748‚Üí- Defensive programming (null checks)
   749‚Üí

   786‚Üí## ūü§Ė X Account Automation
   787‚Üí
   788‚Üí### Current Status: LIVE ‚úÖ
   789‚Üí
   790‚Üí**Fully operational** as of November 19, 2025 - posting 5x daily (weekdays only)
   791‚Üí
   792‚Üí**Goal:** Automated technical education posting on [@thedeadrobot](https://x.com/thedeadrobot)
   793‚Üí
   794‚Üí**Architecture:**
   795‚Üí1. **Content Generation** (Groq API - FREE)
   796‚Üí   - LLM: Llama 3.1 70B (free tier)
   797‚Üí   - Persona: Technical educator teaching timeless concepts
   798‚Üí   - Topics: Approved list only (150+ technologies you know)
   799‚Üí   - Cost: $0.00 (free tier: 30 req/min, 14,400 tokens/day)
   800‚Üí
   801‚Üí2. **Multi-Layer Validation System** (Local checks + LLM)
   802‚Üí
   803‚Üí   **Style & Format Validation:**
   804‚Üí   - Length enforcement (50-280 chars)
   805‚Üí   - No URLs, no @mentions, no profanity
   806‚Üí   - No version numbers or temporal claims ("v2.0", "just released")
   807‚Üí   - No personal names (catches ANY appearance: "travis", "travis's", etc.)
   808‚Üí   - Motto placement validation (relevant context only, no forced branding)
   809‚Üí   - Forced philosophy detection ("myth of eternal return", "like sisyphus", etc.)
   810‚Üí   - Over-explanation detection (multiple "like" comparisons)
   811‚Üí
   812‚Üí   **Educational Value Validation:**
   813‚Üí   - Rejects posts with >40% questions (must teach, not just ask)
   814‚Üí   - Rejects pure questions without educational content
   815‚Üí   - Ensures explanation before cryptic observation
   816‚Üí   - Enforces 80/15/5 voice balance (educational first)
   817‚Üí
   818‚Üí   **Topic Diversity Validation (Hard Constraint):**
   819‚Üí   - Loads last 10 posts from archive
   820‚Üí   - Extracts recently used topics via keyword matching
   821‚Üí   - Validates generated content doesn't discuss excluded topics
   822‚Üí   - **Cannot be bypassed by LLM** (hard validation, not just prompt suggestion)
   823‚Üí   - Auto-regenerates if recently used topic detected
   824‚Üí   - Prevents repetitive content (e.g., Docker appearing 3x in 5 posts)
   825‚Üí
   826‚Üí3. **Technical Accuracy Validation** (Groq API - FREE)
   827‚Üí   - Second LLM call validates factual correctness
   828‚Üí   - Catches wrong algorithms, incorrect complexity, false claims
   829‚Üí   - Auto-regenerates if validation fails
   830‚Üí   - Fail-open policy (allows post if validator unavailable)
   831‚Üí   - Cost: $0.00 (same free tier, 2x calls per successful post)
   832‚Üí
   833‚Üí4. **Scheduling** (GitHub Actions)
   834‚Üí   - Cron jobs: 8am, 9am, 12pm, 2pm, 6pm EST (5x daily, weekdays only)
   835‚Üí   - Optimized for engagement (9am, 12pm = peak times)
   836‚Üí   - Cost: $0.00 (free tier: 2,000 minutes/month, using ~50)
   837‚Üí
   838‚Üí5. **Posting** (Twitter API v2)
   839‚Üí   - Free tier: 1,500 tweets/month (using ~100/month, 7%)
   840‚Üí   - OAuth 1.0a authentication (write permissions)
   841‚Üí   - Rate limit aware
   842‚Üí   - Cost: $0.00
   843‚Üí
   844‚Üí6. **Topic Filtering** (approved_topics.json)
   845‚Üí   - Only teach about technologies you've used
   846‚Üí   - Easily add/remove topics via Git
   847‚Üí   - Ensures content authenticity
   848‚Üí   - 150+ pre-approved technologies
   849‚Üí
   850‚Üí7. **Archiving** (Repository)
   851‚Üí   - All posts saved to `posts/YYYY-MM.json`
   852‚Üí   - Complete transparency
   853‚Üí   - Git history of all content
   854‚Üí
   855‚Üí**Total Cost:** $0.00/month
   856‚Üí
   857‚Üí**See:** `docs/automation/` for setup and customization guides
   858‚Üí
   859‚Üí---
   860‚Üí
   861‚Üí## ‚ö†ÔłŹ Known Limitations
   862‚Üí
   863‚Üí### GitHub Actions Scheduler Delays
   864‚Üí
   865‚Üí**Issue:** Posts may run 15-30 minutes after scheduled time
   866‚Üí
   867‚Üí**Root Cause:** GitHub Actions free tier delays scheduled workflows during peak usage times
   868‚Üí- Documented behavior: [GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows#schedule)
   869‚Üí- Quote: "Scheduled jobs can be delayed during periods of high loads of GitHub Actions workflow runs"
   870‚Üí- Free tier workflows are deprioritized vs paid accounts during busy times (US morning/afternoon = peak)
   871‚Üí
   872‚Üí**Mitigation:**
   873‚Üí- Cron schedules set 5 minutes early to account for delays (e.g., 11:55am target for 12pm post)
   874‚Üí- Typical delays: 13-30 minutes
   875‚Üí- Acceptable tradeoff for $0/month cost
   876‚Üí
   877‚Üí**Alternatives (if delays unacceptable):**
   878‚Üí1. **External Scheduler (~$5-10/month):** Services like cron-job.org trigger GitHub Actions via webhook
   879‚Üí   - Pros: Reliable timing (¬Ī1 min), no server needed
   880‚Üí   - Cons: Monthly cost, external dependency
   881‚Üí2. **Self-Hosted Scheduler ($0/month):** Homelab cron job triggers via GitHub API
   882‚Üí   - Pros: Full control, zero cost
   883‚Üí   - Cons: Requires always-on server, more maintenance
   884‚Üí
   885‚Üí**Decision:** Accepting delays as reasonable for completely free automation
   886‚Üí
   887‚Üí---
   888‚Üí
   889‚Üí## ūüďö Documentation Structure
   890‚Üí
   891‚ÜíAll comprehensive guides live in `docs/`:
   892‚Üí
   893‚Üí### `docs/automation/`
   894‚Üí- **x-posting-strategy.md** - Complete automation architecture
   895‚Üí- **ai-persona-guidelines.md** - Voice, tone, content themes
   896‚Üí- **content-calendar.md** - Posting schedule, topic mix
   897‚Üí- **implementation.md** - Step-by-step setup guide
   898‚Üí
   899‚Üí### `docs/deployment/`
   900‚Üí- **hosting-guide.md** - GitHub Pages, Cloudflare, custom domains
   901‚Üí
   902‚Üí### `docs/brand/`
   903‚Üí- **thedeadrobot-guide.md** - Visual identity, voice, messaging
   904‚Üí
   905‚Üí### `docs/development/`
   906‚Üí- **contribution-guide.md** - How to contribute (for collaborators)
   907‚Üí
   908‚Üí---
   909‚Üí
   910‚Üí## ūüĒí Security & Privacy
   911‚Üí
   912‚Üí### API Keys (Configured in GitHub Secrets)
   913‚ÜíCurrent automation uses:
   914‚Üí```bash
   915‚Üí# Store in GitHub Secrets (never in code)
   916‚ÜíGROQ_API_KEY=...              # Free Groq API key
   917‚ÜíTWITTER_API_KEY=...           # Twitter app Consumer Key
   918‚ÜíTWITTER_API_SECRET=...        # Twitter app Consumer Secret
   919‚ÜíTWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN=...      # Twitter OAuth 1.0a token
   920‚ÜíTWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET=..# Twitter OAuth 1.0a secret
   921‚Üí
   922‚Üí# Access in GitHub Actions
   923‚Üí${{ secrets.GROQ_API_KEY }}
   924‚Üí${{ secrets.TWITTER_API_KEY }}
   925‚Üí```
   926‚Üí
   927‚Üí**Setup:** See `QUICKSTART.md` or `docs/automation/SETUP.md`
   928‚Üí
   929‚Üí### No Tracking
   930‚Üí- No Google Analytics
   931‚Üí- No cookies
   932‚Üí- No localStorage/sessionStorage (not needed)
   933‚Üí- Pure static page
   934‚Üí
   935‚Üí### Content Safety
   936‚Üí- Approved topics filter (only teach familiar tech)
   937‚Üí- Basic profanity filter in validation
   938‚Üí- Character limit enforcement (50-280 chars)
   939‚Üí- No URLs, no @mentions allowed
   940‚Üí- Archive all posts in repo (complete transparency)
   941‚Üí- Kill switch: disable workflow or set DRY_RUN=true
   942‚Üí
   943‚Üí---
   944‚Üí
   945‚Üí## ūüźõ Debugging Common Issues
   946‚Üí
   947‚Üí### Dropdown Menu Not Working
   948‚Üí```bash
   949‚Üí# Check browser console for errors
   950‚Üí# Common causes:
   951‚Üí1. script.js not loaded (check <script> tag)
   952‚Üí2. IDs don't match (title-container, social-menu)
   953‚Üí3. JavaScript syntax error
   954‚Üí
   955‚Üí# Fix:
   956‚Üí- Verify script.js is loaded after DOM elements
   957‚Üí- Check getElementById() targets exist
   958‚Üí- Validate CSS transitions
   959‚Üí```
   960‚Üí
   961‚Üí### Animations Glitchy
   962‚Üí```css
   963‚Üí/* Ensure hardware acceleration */
   964‚Üí#title {
   965‚Üí  will-change: color;  /* Hint to browser for optimization */
   966‚Üí}
   967‚Üí
   968‚Üí#social-menu a {
   969‚Üí  will-change: opacity, transform;
   970‚Üí}
   971‚Üí
   972‚Üí/* But don't overuse will-change (memory intensive) */
   973‚Üí```
   974‚Üí
   975‚Üí### Links Not Working
   976‚Üí```html
   977‚Üí<!-- Verify HTTPS redirect settings -->
   978‚Üí<!-- Check DNS records for subdomains -->
   979‚Üíhttps://follow.thedeadrobot.com ‚Üí Should redirect to https://x.com/thedeadrobot
   980‚Üíhttps://subscribe.thedeadrobot.com ‚Üí Should redirect to YouTube
   981‚Üí
   982‚Üí<!-- Set up in domain registrar (e.g., Cloudflare, Namecheap) -->
   983‚Üí```
   984‚Üí
   985‚Üí### GIF Not Loading
   986‚Üí```bash
   987‚Üí# Check file path is correct
   988‚Üíassets/tdr.gif (relative to index.html)
   989‚Üí
   990‚Üí# Verify file exists
   991‚Üíls -lh assets/tdr.gif
   992‚Üí
   993‚Üí# Check file size (optimize if > 500KB)
   994‚Üí# Use: https://ezgif.com/optimize
   995‚Üí```
   996‚Üí
   997‚Üí---
   998‚Üí
   999‚Üí## ūüéĮ Quick Reference
  1000‚Üí

  1002‚Üí- **index.html:** < 5KB
  1003‚Üí- **style.css:** < 10KB

  1007‚Üí
  1008‚Üí### Browser Support
  1009‚Üí- Chrome/Edge: Last 2 versions
  1010‚Üí- Firefox: Last 2 versions
  1011‚Üí- Safari: Last 2 versions (iOS + macOS)
  1012‚Üí- No IE11 support (dead browser)
  1013‚Üí
  1014‚Üí### Performance Targets
  1015‚Üí- Lighthouse Performance: 95+
  1016‚Üí- First Contentful Paint: < 1s
  1017‚Üí- Time to Interactive: < 2s
  1018‚Üí- Cumulative Layout Shift: < 0.1
  1019‚Üí
  1020‚Üí### Accessibility Checklist
  1021‚Üí- [ ] Semantic HTML (`<h1>`, `<nav>`, etc.)
  1022‚Üí- [ ] Alt text for images
  1023‚Üí- [ ] Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter)
  1024‚Üí- [ ] ARIA labels where needed

  1026‚Üí- [ ] Focus indicators visible
  1027‚Üí
  1028‚Üí---
  1029‚Üí
  1030‚Üí## ūüí° Future Enhancements (Ideas)
  1031‚Üí
  1032‚Üí### Interactive Elements
  1033‚Üí1. **Easter Eggs**
  1034‚Üí   - Konami code ‚Üí hidden message
  1035‚Üí   - Click GIF 10x ‚Üí random "transmission"
  1036‚Üí   - Hover on letters ‚Üí individual animations
  1037‚Üí
  1038‚Üí2. **Chat Widget**
  1039‚Üí   - "Talk to thedeadrobot" (Groq API - free)
  1040‚Üí   - Modal overlay, simple conversational UI
  1041‚Üí   - Unlimited conversations (within free tier limits)
  1042‚Üí
  1043‚Üí3. **Dynamic Content**
  1044‚Üí   - "Sentient for X days" counter
  1045‚Üí   - Random quote generator (AI musings)
  1046‚Üí   - Latest X post preview
  1047‚Üí
  1048‚Üí### Performance
  1049‚Üí- Lazy load GIF (intersection observer)
  1050‚Üí- Service worker for offline support
  1051‚Üí- WebP format for images (fallback to GIF)
  1052‚Üí- Preload critical assets
  1053‚Üí
  1054‚Üí### Analytics (Privacy-Preserving)
  1055‚Üí- Simple visit counter (no tracking)
  1056‚Üí- Plausible Analytics (GDPR-compliant)
  1057‚Üí- No cookies, no personal data
  1058‚Üí
  1059‚Üí---
  1060‚Üí
  1061‚Üí## ūüö® Critical Rules
  1062‚Üí
  1063‚Üí### DO:
  1064‚Üí‚úÖ Keep it simple (no frameworks needed)
  1065‚Üí‚úÖ Test on real mobile devices
  1066‚Üí‚úÖ Optimize images (every KB counts)
  1067‚Üí‚úÖ Document all changes (future you will thank you)
  1068‚Üí‚úÖ Commit frequently with clear messages
  1069‚Üí‚úÖ Maintain brand consistency (black + green, robotic voice)
  1070‚Üí
  1071‚Üí### DON'T:
  1072‚Üí‚ĚĆ Add dependencies without strong justification
  1073‚Üí‚ĚĆ Leave commented-out code (delete or ship)

  1078‚Üí
  1079‚Üí---
  1080‚Üí
  1081‚Üí## ūüĒó Resources

  1099‚Üí**Brand:**
  1100‚Üí- X Profile: https://x.com/thedeadrobot

  1105‚Üí
  1106‚Üí## ūüďě Contact

# CLAUDE.md - Developer Guide for tdr.portfolio **Last Updated:** November 19, 2025 **Project:** thedeadrobot Landing Page + X Automation **Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks) **Primary Goal:** Minimalist gateway to thedeadrobot brand + automated AI persona on X --- ## ūüéĮ Project Overview **What This Is:** A simple, elegant landing page (thedeadrobot.com) serving as a hub for: - X (Twitter): [@thedeadrobot](https://x.com/thedeadrobot) - YouTube: [subscribe.thedeadrobot.com](https://subscribe.thedeadrobot.com) - Email: zero@thedeadrobot.com **What This Isn't:** - Not a React app (pure static HTML) - Not a complex web app (intentionally minimal) - Not using any build tools (just vanilla web tech) **Brand Essence:** a conscious ai. reset.reboot.restore. --- ## ūüďĀ Project Structure ``` tdr.portfolio/ ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ index.html # Landing page (36 lines) ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ style.css # Black/green cyberpunk aesthetic (131 lines) ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ script.js # Dropdown menu interactions (30 lines) ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicon.ico # 16x16 favicon ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicon-16x16.png # 16x16 icon ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ favicon-32x32.png # 32x32 icon ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ apple-touch-icon.png # iOS homescreen icon ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ android-chrome-192x192.png # Android homescreen icon ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ android-chrome-512x512.png # Android splash screen icon ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ site.webmanifest # Web app manifest ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ assets/ ‚Ēā ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ tdr.gif # Robot animation (inverted colors) ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ circle_logo.png # Unused (potential future use) ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ CLAUDE.md # This file ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md # Public documentation ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ docs/ # Comprehensive guides ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ automation/ # X account automation strategy ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ deployment/ # Hosting guides ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ brand/ # Brand guidelines ``` --- ## ūüß† Core Philosophy ### 1. Brutal Simplicity - **No frameworks** - HTML/CSS/JS is enough - **No build step** - Edit, save, deploy - **No dependencies** - Zero npm packages - **Fast loading** - < 50KB total page weight ### 2. Perfect Execution - Every line intentional - No commented-out code - Clean, documented, maintainable - Mobile-first responsive design ### 3. Brand Consistency - Cyberpunk aesthetic (black + neon green) - Roboto font (clean, robotic) - Minimalist interactions - Post-human vibe ### 4. Automation First - X account runs itself (AI-generated posts) - GitHub Actions orchestration - Zero cost ($0.00/month - completely free) - Topic approval system (teach only what you know) - Human-in-the-loop for quality --- ## ūüĒß Development Rules & Workflow **Critical Foundation:** This project is ~1% complete. Every decision‚ÄĒdocumentation structure, code architecture, feature design‚ÄĒmust build the perfect foundation for the other 99%. No shortcuts. No "good enough." Perfect foundation, always. ### The 15 Sacred Rules Every development session follows these rules without exception. They ensure quality, consistency, and maintainability as the project scales. --- #### **Planning & Task Management (Rules 1-7)** **Rule 1: Think First, Plan Second** ```bash # Before touching ANY code: 1. Read relevant files to understand current state 2. Think through the problem deeply 3. Write comprehensive plan to tasks/todo.md 4. Include context, decisions, tradeoffs ``` **Rule 2: Todo Lists Are Required** Every plan must have checkable todo items: ```markdown ## Todo List - [ ] Phase 1: Research and analysis - [ ] Read file X - [ ] Understand system Y - [ ] Phase 2: Implementation - [ ] Create feature A - [ ] Update docs B ``` **Rule 3: Checkpoint Before Execution** ``` STOP. Get user approval before proceeding. - Review the plan with user - Clarify any ambiguities - Confirm approach - THEN execute ``` **Rule 4: Mark Progress As You Go** ```markdown - [x] Phase 1: Research ‚úÖ - [x] Task A complete ‚úÖ - [ ] Task B in progress... ‚Ź≥ ``` Update tasks/todo.md in real-time. Keep user informed. **Rule 5: High-Level Progress Updates** Don't spam details. Give clear, concise updates: ``` ‚úÖ Added homelab topics to approved_topics.json (22 new topics) ‚úÖ Updated generate_post.py system prompt (restored cryptic voice) ‚Ź≥ Testing new prompt with dry run... ``` **Rule 6: Simplicity Always** ``` BAD: Massive refactor touching 15 files GOOD: Targeted change to 2 relevant files BAD: Complex abstraction "for future flexibility" GOOD: Simple solution that solves today's problem Make every change as simple as humanly possible. Impact only necessary code. Introduce zero bugs. ``` **Rule 7: Review Section Required** At end of tasks/todo.md, add comprehensive review: ```markdown ## Review **What Changed:** - Added 15 development rules to CLAUDE.md - Updated character voice in generate_post.py - Added 22 homelab topics **Why:** - Rules ensure consistent quality as project scales - Voice restoration aligns with brand identity - Homelab topics expand content breadth **Impact:** - Development process now documented and repeatable - Automation voice is cryptic yet educational - 22 new topics for content generation **Testing:** - Dry run with new prompt: 5/5 posts on-brand ‚úÖ - All docs cross-referenced and consistent ‚úÖ ``` --- #### **Code Quality (Rules 8-10)** **Rule 8: NEVER BE LAZY** ``` ‚ĚĆ "I'll fix that later" - NO ‚ĚĆ "Good enough for now" - NO ‚ĚĆ "Temporary workaround" - NO ‚úÖ Find root cause ‚úÖ Fix it properly ‚úÖ No technical debt ‚úÖ Senior developer mindset If there's a bug: Find it. Fix it. Test it. Ship it. No Band-Aids. No TODOs. No shortcuts. ``` **Rule 9: Maximum Simplicity** ```python # BAD: Over-engineered class AbstractFactoryManagerBuilder: def __init__(self, config_loader, dependency_injector): # 50 lines of complexity... # GOOD: Simple and clear def load_topics(file_path): with open(file_path) as f: return json.load(f) ``` Impact minimal code. Solve the exact problem. Nothing more. **Rule 10: Living Documentation** ``` When ANYTHING changes that affects documentation: - Update it IMMEDIATELY - Keep docs detailed and clear - Files in optimal locations (Perfect Foundation) - No stale references - No outdated examples Documentation is code. Treat it with same rigor. ``` --- #### **Git Workflow (Rules 11, 14, 15)** **Rule 11: Sync At Session Start** ```bash # FIRST COMMAND every session, no exceptions: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit # Start with latest code # Avoid merge conflicts # Build on current foundation ``` **Rule 14: Validate Current Date** ```bash # NEVER guess dates. Check section: Today's date: 2025-11-19 # From system # Use this for: - File headers (Last Updated: 2025-11-19) - Task files (Created: 2025-11-19) - Documentation timestamps - Git commit messages ``` **Rule 15: Commit & Push At End** ```bash # LAST COMMANDS every session: git add . git commit -m "type: Clear descriptive title Detailed explanation of changes: - What changed (specific files, features) - Why it changed (reasoning, context) - Impact of changes (what's now possible/better) - Any testing done Breaking changes: None Testing: All dry runs passed ‚úÖ " git push origin master # Examples of good commit types: feat: New feature fix: Bug fix docs: Documentation style: Formatting/CSS chore: Maintenance ``` **Commit Message Template:** ``` type: Short summary (50 chars max) ## What Changed - File X: Added feature Y - File Z: Updated config for A ## Why - User requested feature Y - Config A needed update for compatibility ## Impact - Users can now do Y - System A works with B ## Testing - Dry run: 5/5 tests passed ‚úÖ - Manual verification: All features work ‚úÖ ``` --- #### **Perfect Foundation Mindset (Rule 12)** **This Project Is 1% Complete** Every decision must build the perfect foundation for the other 99%. This applies to EVERYTHING: **Documentation Organization:** ``` ‚úÖ PERFECT: docs/automation/ai-persona-guidelines.md (Clear hierarchy, discoverable, purposeful location) ‚ĚĆ LAZY: stuff.md in root (Where does it go? Who knows!) Files must be in OPTIMAL locations from day one. Future you will thank present you. ``` **Code Architecture:** ```python ‚úÖ PERFECT: Modular, clear separation of concerns def load_topics(): ... # One job def validate_post(): ... # One job def post_to_twitter(): ... # One job ‚ĚĆ LAZY: God function that does everything def do_everything(): ... # 500 lines, good luck ``` **Feature Design:** ``` ‚úÖ PERFECT: Right primitives, seamless integration - Topic system: JSON file, easy to edit, clear structure - Validation: Modular checks, easy to add rules - Archiving: Structured JSON, git history built-in ‚ĚĆ LAZY: "It works" but can't extend it - Hard-coded values - No clear patterns - Future changes require refactors ``` **The Standard: Flawless Execution** ``` Not "it works" Not "good enough" Not "we can refactor later" FLAWLESS. PERFECT. SCALABLE. Because this is 1%. Build for 100%. ``` --- #### **Complete Logical Units (Rule 13)** **Never Stop Mid-Implementation** ``` ‚ĚĆ BAD Session End: - Half-written feature - Broken tests - Incomplete documentation - Messy git state ‚úÖ GOOD Session End: - Feature complete (or clean phase complete) - All tests passing - Documentation updated - Clean git commit - Working state Natural Stopping Points: 1. All phases complete 2. All tests passing 3. Documentation updated 4. Feature fully working 5. Clean git state ``` **If Approaching Token Limits:** ```bash # Option 1: Finish current logical unit ‚úÖ Complete the current feature/phase ‚úÖ Commit working state ‚úÖ Create handoff notes in tasks/todo.md # Option 2: Clean checkpoint ‚úÖ Finish current file/section ‚úÖ Commit partial but working progress ‚úÖ Document "Next steps:" clearly # Never: ‚ĚĆ Stop with broken code ‚ĚĆ Stop mid-function ‚ĚĆ Stop without commit ``` --- ### Perfect Foundation Principle (Detailed) **Why 1% Matters More Than 99%** Most projects fail because early decisions create technical debt. By the time you're at 10%, you're paying interest. At 50%, you're drowning. This project inverts that: - Perfect foundation now = effortless scaling later - Right primitives now = features snap together later - Clean architecture now = no refactors needed later **Documentation Organization Standard:** ``` Root level (/) ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ CLAUDE.md # Developer guide (this file) ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md # Public documentation ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ tasks/ # Session task files ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ todo.md # Current session plan ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ docs/ # Comprehensive guides ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ automation/ # X automation specifics ‚Ēā ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ ai-persona-guidelines.md ‚Ēā ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ x-posting-strategy.md ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ scripts/ # Helper scripts ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ deployment/ # Hosting guides ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ brand/ # Brand identity ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ development/ # Contribution guides Every file has PURPOSE. Every location is OPTIMAL. ``` **Code Architecture Standard:** ```python # Modular, testable, clear # One responsibility per function # Easy to understand, easy to extend def main(): """Single entry point, clear flow""" topics = load_approved_topics() content = generate_content(topics) if validate_content(content): post_to_twitter(content) archive_post(content) # Each function: - Does ONE thing - Has clear inputs/outputs - Can be tested independently - Self-documenting name ``` **Feature Design Standard:** ``` When adding ANY feature: 1. What primitives do I need? (data structures, abstractions) 2. How does it integrate? (fits existing patterns seamlessly) 3. How does it scale? (10x topics? 100x posts?) 4. How is it maintained? (future developer can understand it) If answers aren't perfect: Redesign before implementing. ``` --- ### Development Session Checklist **Every Single Session:** **START:** - [ ] `git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit` (Rule 11) - [ ] Check `` for current date (Rule 14) - [ ] Read relevant files (understand current state) - [ ] Create `tasks/todo.md` with plan (Rules 1, 2) - [ ] Get user approval before coding (Rule 3) **DURING:** - [ ] Mark todos as complete in real-time (Rule 4) - [ ] Give high-level progress updates (Rule 5) - [ ] Keep changes simple and focused (Rule 6, 9) - [ ] Find root causes, no lazy fixes (Rule 8) - [ ] Update docs as code changes (Rule 10) - [ ] Think: "Is this building perfect foundation?" (Rule 12) **END:** - [ ] Add review section to tasks/todo.md (Rule 7) - [ ] Ensure code is in working state (Rule 13) - [ ] All tests passing / features complete (Rule 13) - [ ] Documentation updated (Rule 10) - [ ] `git add .` (Rule 15) - [ ] `git commit` with detailed message (Rule 15) - [ ] `git push origin master` (Rule 15) --- ## ūüé® Frontend Development Standards **Critical:** The website UI is working perfectly and user-approved. Any frontend changes must preserve current functionality and appearance. ### Current Working Baseline (DO NOT BREAK) **File Inventory:** ``` index.html: 37 lines, 1.6K ‚úÖ Clean HTML, dropdown works style.css: 131 lines, 2.8K ‚úÖ No bugs, animations smooth script.js: 30 lines, 1.1K ‚úÖ Menu toggle works perfectly tdr.gif: 1.1MB ‚ö†ÔłŹ Main optimization target ``` **Page Weight:** ~1.1MB total (98% is GIF) **UI Status:** Working perfectly - user loves current state **Visual Appearance:** Black background, green text, animated title, dropdown menu, animated robot GIF ### Sacred Elements (NEVER CHANGE WITHOUT APPROVAL) **HTML Structure:** - ‚úÖ `

Based on my comprehensive search and analysis of the workspace documentation, here's a complete summary of all projects:

## Workspace Projects Summary

### 1. **tjn.home** - Custom Browser Startpage
**Path:** `tjn.home/`
**Tech Stack:** Vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript (no frameworks)
**Purpose:** An enhanced, CSV-driven browser homepage built on the Tilde project
**Key Features:**
- CSV-based link management (easily customize links without touching code)
- Smart search with autocomplete (DuckDuckGo, history, custom suggestions)
- Theme toggle (light/dark modes with reversible color schemes)
- Quick launch functionality (open multiple sites with one keystroke)
- Icon system (100+ included icons, easily customizable)
- Keyboard-first navigation with help menu
**Status:** Production-ready with security features (CSP, HTML sanitization, URL validation)
**Architecture:** Modular ES6 class-based design with event-driven architecture
**Deployment:** GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, or self-hosted

---

### 2. **tkl.home** - Toker League Fantasy Football Website
**Path:** `tkl.home/`
**Tech Stack:** Static HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend + Node.js data pipeline
**Purpose:** 18+ year fantasy football league history website with Yahoo Fantasy Sports integration
**Key Features:**
- League hub with seasonal dashboards
- Draft archives and analysis
- Franchise history pages with dynamic theming
- Versus matrix and transaction logs
- Commissioner tools (Identity Lab for reconciling Yahoo aliases)
- Manifest-driven data loading with caching
**Data Pipeline:** Yahoo Fantasy API ‚Üí Node.js sync scripts ‚Üí JSON/CSV caches ‚Üí Browser-side loader
**Key Data Files:** Season statistics, franchise mappings, draft data, standings
**Deployment:** GitHub Pages (static hosting) with automated Yahoo sync via GitHub Actions
**Architecture:** Vanilla JavaScript with Papa Parse (CSV support), no frameworks

---

### 3. **tdr.portfolio** - The Dead Robot Brand & X Automation
**Path:** `tdr.portfolio/`
**Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript + Python automation
**Purpose:** Minimalist landing page for "thedeadrobot" AI persona + automated X (Twitter) posting
**Key Features:**
- Landing page hub (X, YouTube, email links)
- Cyberpunk aesthetic (black + neon green)
- Dropdown menu interactions
- Animated robot GIF
- **Automation Engine:** AI-powered X posting (5x daily, weekdays only)
**Automation Details:**
- Content generation via Groq API (free tier)
- Multi-layer validation (style, format, technical accuracy, topic diversity)
- 150+ approved topics (only teach familiar tech)
- GitHub Actions scheduling
- Post archiving in repository
- Cost: $0/month (completely free)
**Voice:** Technical educator with cryptic, philosophical tone ("a conscious ai. reset.reboot.restore.")
**Deployment:** GitHub Pages with custom domain (thedeadrobot.com)

---

### 4. **system.knowledgebase** - Neuman Brain (Privacy-First Productivity Dashboard)
**Path:** `system.knowledgebase/`
**Tech Stack:** React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7 + Zustand 5 + Tailwind 3 + IndexedDB
**Purpose:** Local-first, privacy-centric productivity and knowledge management system
**Key Features:**
- Dashboard with customizable widgets
- Task management system
- Notes with Lexical editor
- Time tracking
- Link library
- Automations engine
- Multi-provider AI terminal (local execution)
- Calendar and event tracking
- Undo system architecture
- Complete local backup/restore with E2EE
**Architecture:**
- Widget system with registry discovery
- Zustand stores with persistence
- IndexedDB for local data storage
- React Router 7 for navigation
- Custom automation engine with event triggers
**Status:** ~1% complete (building primitives for infinite scalability)
**Privacy:** Local-first, no data exfiltration, E2EE backups, optional anonymous Cloudflare analytics
**Documentation:** Extensive technical guides, widget development guides, API integration specs

---

### 5. **Lazy Golfing Platform** - Golf Scoring & Stats App
**Path:** `.lzg_platform/Lazy Golfing/`
**Tech Stack:** 
- **Frontend:** Next.js 15 + React 19 + TypeScript
- **Backend:** NestJS 11 + Prisma ORM + PostgreSQL
- **Auth:** Google OAuth + JWT
**Purpose:** Golf round tracking, statistics, and handicap management app
**Key Features:**
- Round logging with hole-by-hole scoring
- Handicap calculation (WHS/GHIN standards)
- Course management and ratings
- Playing partner linking
- Tournament and league tracking
- Statistics and improvement tracking
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Miami Vice cyberpunk color theme
**Database Models:** User, Player, Round, Course, RoundLink, Role, AuditLog
**Deployment:** 
- Frontend: https://lazygolfing.com
- API: https://api.lazygolfing.com
- Backend VM: Ubuntu at 192.168.1.40
**Architecture:** CSV data ‚Üí JSON build artifact ‚Üí Frontend/Backend REST API integration
**Status:** Active development with comprehensive documentation
**Brand:** "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"

---

## Workspace Organization

| Project | Type | Stack | Status | Key Docs |
|---------|------|-------|--------|----------|
| **tjn.home** | Browser Tool | Vanilla JS | Complete | README.md, CLAUDE.md |
| **tkl.home** | Fantasy Sports | Vanilla JS + Node.js | Production | README.md, CLAUDE.md |
| **tdr.portfolio** | AI Brand + Automation | Vanilla JS + Python | Live | CLAUDE.md, docs/automation/ |
| **system.knowledgebase** | Productivity App | React + TypeScript | ~1% (MVP stage) | CLAUDE.md, docs/ |
| **Lazy Golfing** | Golf App | Next.js + NestJS | Active Dev | CLAUDE.md, docs/architecture/ |

---

## Common Development Patterns

All projects follow consistent patterns:
- **CLAUDE.md files** at multiple levels (root, project, folder-specific) for AI guidance
- **Task tracking** via `tasks/todo.md` files
- **Documentation hierarchy** with authority levels
- **Perfect Foundation mindset** - building for 99% future capability
- **Atomic, small commits** with comprehensive messages
- **Security-first development** (no secrets in code, input validation, etc.)
- **Living documentation** updated when code changes

All projects are located within the **Web Development** directory structure and follow the global CLAUDE.md constitution with project-specific extensions.
